title: 回到司徒基地
body: //畫面暗掉亮起，主角已經做在斯圖基地的椅子上，程芳也在旁邊兩人對視，隆生從坊間走出來。 Lawrence Chang: I got in touch with 23 and Nin. Frances Cheng: How are they? Lawrence Chang: They're tracking down Chameleon. Lawrence Chang: Nin has called in support from OWL. They're forming a full circle and homing in. Lawrence Chang: He can't possibly slip through. Lawrence Chang: We will settle the score for you, Ms. Cheng!;<> Frances Cheng: Thank you.;<> Lawrence Chang: 23 is on her way back.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Doesn't Situ need her? Lawrence Chang: There are other OWL agents. ->When is she arriving? Lawrence Chang: She didn't say. Lawrence Chang: But it won't be long. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Nin prefers 23 at the base, said it's safer.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...I guess she just doesn't trust me to hold the fort alone.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Hey, there's nothing wrong with being a rookie.;//可以記錄 ->Surely she does not think of you like that.;//可以記錄 Lawrence Chang: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: That means...;<>;<> Frances Cheng: What happened today?;<> Frances Cheng: Is it just me, or did you guys run into something terrible? Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Thought: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: We did. Lawrence Chang: Everything could go wrong did go wrong.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: The original plan was to evacuate all the children before the real deal shows up. Frances Cheng: The real deal?;<> Lawrence Chang: Eh...;<> Lawrence Chang: Chameleon and his mercenaries. Frances Cheng: I see. How did that go?;<> Lawrence Chang: Turns out the whole transaction was a trap set by Chameleon.;<> Lawrence Chang: Nin, Helper, and Jason were surrounded basically as soon as they went in. Frances Cheng: Wait, what? Jason was on the front line? Why?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I was sent inside for a quick recon mission. // ->Well, I'm where everyone needs me to be. Frances Cheng: What's that supposed to mean? Thought: Fran's angry. Lawrence Chang: Um, it's... Nin sent Jason inside for reconnaissance. <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: We couldn't see what's inside, so, you know...;<> Frances Cheng: Why you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Situ's order. ->It was supposed to be safe. ->We didn't have anyone else. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: But...!;<> Lawrence Chang: But it should have been Lawrence's job.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Right? Ms. Cheng? Frances Cheng: I didn't...;<> Lawrence Chang: It's OK. I thought it should have been me, too. Still do.;<> Lawrence Chang: I have no idea why Nin sent Jason. Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Most likely, probably she doesn't trust me enough?;<> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Thought: ...The air is dripping condensed awkwardness.;<> Lawrence Chang: Anyway, this is what happened on my side.;<> Lawrence Chang: Jason, Nin, and Helper, they went into the construction site. We kept in contact over the radio.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Then the radio was somehow cut off. Lawrence Chang: Right away, 23 realized there's a jammer nearby. It could be a trap. Lawrence Chang: But the ball was already rolling. What can we do? She tried to bypass the jammer instead. Lawrence Chang: Outside, all gangsters evacuated the site, and there were mercenaries guarding their vans.;<> Lawrence Chang: By then we were pretty damn sure it's a trap. Lawrence Chang: We did what we could—watch, analyze, and keep trying to reestablish contact with everyone. Lawrence Chang: There was a gunfight. All mercenaries rushed in for reinforcement.;<> Lawrence Chang: Since their vans were left unguarded, we thought maybe we could get closer for a look. Lawrence Chang: Then a whole battalion of Chameleon's men arrived. Lawrence Chang: Half of them went into the site, the rest guarded the vans.;<> Lawrence Chang: I was scared. I thought maybe Jason, you could be caught or...;<> Frances Cheng: ...Or dead.;<> Lawrence Chang: Yes.;<> Lawrence Chang: 23 wanted to follow them inside. I managed to hold her back.;<> Lawrence Chang: After a while, we managed to reach Nin over the radio.;<> Lawrence Chang: The signal's terrible. We barely heard what she said over the noise.;<> Lawrence Chang: She didn't say much, though. Just that the situation was critical. She told us to take their vans and run when it's time. Lawrence Chang: She didn't get to explain what "it's time" was supposed to mean, when the radio's cut off again. Lawrence Chang: After a while, most of the mercenaries around the van went inside, too, leaving behind only three of them.;<> Lawrence Chang: After that, a lot of shooting, like there's a war going on inside the site. Thought: That should be Situ vs Chameleon & gang...;<> Lawrence Chang: We moved over to give Nin a hand. Even she couldn't handle that many men at the same time. Lawrence Chang: Together, we took out the three guards...;<> Lawrence Chang: And 23 stopped me just when we were about to join the war.;<> Lawrence Chang: She said this had to be the "time" that Nin said. Lawrence Chang: We should take the vans and run. By the way, the children were inside the vans alright. Lawrence Chang: We had a fight on the spot, with me insisting we should help Nin.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: She didn't agree. Said we must follow Nin's plan. Lawrence Chang: I...;<> Lawrence Chang: I could tell 23 wanted more than anyone else to be there with Nin, but she didn't say it.;<> Lawrence Chang: So she left me there to keep watch, and drove both vans to our contact waiting at the rendezvous. <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Your contact? Lawrence Chang: Some friend of 23 and Nin's in the Shithole. Lawrence Chang: They will take the children to safety. ->She drove two vans, by herself? How? Lawrence Chang: Easy enough. Set one of them on auto follow mode. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: I wanted to either go with her, or go inside to lend Nin a hand. I had to do something. Lawrence Chang: ...She said no. Told me again to stand my ground. Lawrence Chang: Said once you guys break out, I should have our own van running and ready to bolt. Lawrence Chang: But I know what she's really thinking.;<> Lawrence Chang: She thought if Nin and Helper failed, I could at least save myself. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: So I went back to our van, and watched the site exit from afar. Lawrence Chang: Then there came this BOOM!;<> Lawrence Chang: A huge explosion like someone nuked the place. Frances Cheng: Explosion!?;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Yep. Right after the explosion, Nin emerged, followed by Jason.;<> Lawrence Chang: That's my side of the story. Frances Cheng: Dear gods...;<>;//芳把臉摀起來受不了的感覺 Thought: Fran... Lawrence Chang: Over to you, Jason. What was it like inside the site?;<> Lawrence Chang: I mean, there must be a lot going on.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Chameleon knocked me out and pinned me down.;//這要記錄 Frances Cheng: Ms. Situ didn't come to your rescue?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She and Helper were kept busy elsewhere. ->She couldn't make it. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Meaning...;<> Frances Cheng: Chameleon used you as his hostage? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sort of. ->He used me as bait. <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: So Nin engaged Chameleon because of you...;<> Lawrence Chang: She fought the whole mercenary group because there was no choice.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->We did have a choice. We could have pulled out.;<> Frances Cheng: ...Pull out? Frances Cheng: But you were...? ->... <<#EndSimple>> <>;<> ->I'd rather leave the storytelling to Situ herself. Lawrence Chang: But— Frances Cheng: Give him a break, Lawrence. Please.;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: You're right. Sorry. <> <<#EndSimple>> //23突然衝回來，不發一語快速走進房間 <<#End>>

title: 司徒單獨談
body: //司徒走去坐在隆生位子 Frances Cheng: ...Is Lawrence alright?;<> Frances Cheng: He doesn't seem to like your plan... Nin Situ: Like it or not, we follow the plan. Frances Cheng: ...;<> Nin Situ: 23 will talk him out of it. Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: Yes.;<> Nin Situ: I have trust in him. He's not that naïve.;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Even if we don't have a deal with Vision, we can't just publish everything we have.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why? // ->We don't know if there will be anything to publish yet. Nin Situ: True. Nin Situ: There's a big if looming over this whole discussion, that we do manage to get enough information to make Vision think twice. Nin Situ: Let's presume we do. Frances Cheng: Why can't we just publish it? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Because of unpredictable consequences?;<> Nin Situ: Yes.;<> Nin Situ: If we do that, there could be two possible outcomes. Nin Situ: Vision could take a fatal blow. Or maybe it takes the hit and remains standing. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Which one is more likely? Nin Situ: I'd say the latter. ->Fatal blow? There's a chance we can destroy Vision for good? Nin Situ: Yes. Nin Situ: Don't count on the government and its law enforcement. Nin Situ: But Vision's competitors will be all too happy to eat it alive. Nin Situ: Once there's a start, there will be waves and waves of scandals. ->Human experiments are a huge scandal. How could Vision possibly survive that? Nin Situ: Remember Sunrise's "accident" at Test Range 805? Thought: Never heard of it... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nope. Never heard of it. ->Can't say I do. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: My point. Sunrise didn't want people to know. Nin Situ: Leading corporations these days pretty much own the press. They can keep anything under wraps. Nin Situ: Vision, being the cornerstone of social media platforms, can do even more. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: But that is not my point. Nin Situ: I don't want either of these scenarios. Nin Situ: Case one, Vision is destroyed once and for all.;<> Nin Situ: Consider this. AI is the backbone that keeps this society running. As a result, flops are a critical resource. Nin Situ: Suppose Vision disintegrates, its competitors will be all too happy to gobble up the market share left behind. Nin Situ: This will be an excruciating process for many. Nin Situ: Flop farms shut down, or change hands. Workers end up unemployed.;<> Nin Situ: Then there are millions of flop contributors. What will happen to them? Nin Situ: The death of this one corporation could shake the whole nation. Nin Situ: Then we have case two, that Vision takes the blow and survives.;<> Nin Situ: Nothing changes. Human experiments, assassination attempts, all kept in the dark.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->We lose either way. Nin Situ: Exactly.;<> ->Why would Vision want to kill Fran if they have airtight censorship? Nin Situ: There will be stories, rumors. Nin Situ: Possible police investigations, too. Nin Situ: None of this will hurt them, but there's a cost, much higher than having Ms. Cheng removed. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Considering all possibilities, publishing what we have against Vision is a bad idea. Frances Cheng: The stake is too high, and unpredictable. Nin Situ: Yes.;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Actually, keeping our cards without playing them could be beneficial in the long run.;<> Frances Cheng: How so? Nin Situ: Letting them know that we have the info is an effective measure of check and balance. Nin Situ: They will learn to be more cautious going forward, at least think twice before they do anything bad. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see. // ->They will keep doing what they do, just cover it up better. Nin Situ: Yes, and also try not to cross the red line as often. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: We create some sort of sword of Damocles, constantly reminding them of consequences. Nin Situ: I believe this will work.;<> Frances Cheng: You're right.;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: Now, back to the subject of this meeting. Nin Situ: There are things I need to share with you.;<> //if沒有說可以撤退 <<#If($ToldCanRetreat=-1)>> Nin Situ: First of all. Nin Situ: At the construction site, we actually had a chance to pull out before confronting Chameleon. Frances Cheng: Wha...!?;<>;<> Nin Situ: I know. I'm coming to that. Frances Cheng: ...;<> Nin Situ: There's a reason why I chose to engage him instead of pulling out while we could.;<> Nin Situ: The reason is that I believe I could handle the situation. Nin Situ: Moreover. <<#EndIf>> //if說出可以撤退 <<#If($ToldCanRetreat=0)>> Nin Situ: First of all. Nin Situ: Jason, you told 23 and Lawrence that we had a chance to pull out. I'm surprised. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You didn't want them to know? Nin Situ: Yes, for now. ->Excuse me? Nin Situ: No, I'm not blaming you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Nin Situ: I wanted to keep it from them because it could dent the team morale. //if第三日有盡到追問幹嘛司徒要問龍生問題 <<#If($NinExplainWhyWorryRon=0)>> Frances Cheng: Like Lawrence at the Flop Core. Nin Situ: ...Yes.;<> <<#EndIf>> Nin Situ: I know I mean the world to 23. The last thing I want is she panicking at a critical moment like this. Nin Situ: She will be informed, later, when the timing is right. Nin Situ: As to Lawrence...;<> Nin Situ: I keep telling him to keep his cool and stay away from this lone wolf stuff. Nin Situ: Knowing what I did today, he might think bravado is a good thing.;<> Nin Situ: But my decision was not a show of bravado, not on impulse. <<#Simple(10)>> ->You don't think it's on impulse? ->But you did engage Chameleon out of impulse. Nin Situ: I don't see it that way.;<> Nin Situ: It was a calculated decision, factoring in all pros and cons. Nin Situ: I did it because I had a very good chance. ->I see.;<> ->...;<> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: Based on what I have learned...;<> Frances Cheng: You put Jason's life on the line. Are you aware of that? Nin Situ: I am.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Desperate times, desperate measures. Nin Situ: Thank you.;<> Nin Situ: But I did neglect your opinion, which you didn't even have a chance to express. ->Worse still, you didn't ask for my opinion. Nin Situ: ...Yes.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I'm not pointing fingers, Ms. Situ.;<> Frances Cheng: I understand you had to make a decision in a split second. Asking for Jason's opinion was impossible. Frances Cheng: My concern is that, were you really rational like you said?;<> Frances Cheng: Jason's life depends on your decisions. I can't trust you if...;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Like I said, every decision I make is rational.;<> Nin Situ: I had a very good chance to eliminate Chameleon and all his men, on my own. Nin Situ: Otherwise I would have pulled out. Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: Okay. Thought: Fran doesn't look too convinced. Nin Situ: Any other questions about today's mission?;<>;<> Nin Situ: I hope to address your concerns as best as possible. Next we will talk about something else.;<> <> <> <> <> <> <<@第四日問司徒寧循環>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->How much of it did you predict? ($DLStateFloat);//選過消失 <>;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Frances Cheng: What do you mean?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->In the construction site, she said everything was within her expectation. Frances Cheng: That was imp— ->Let her answer, Fran. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: I knew it could be a trap. Frances Cheng: ...But how?;<> Nin Situ: The investigation was too smooth.;<> Nin Situ: The gangster who pointed a gun at Jason and Lawrence in the contract room. We extracted quite a bit of intel from him. Nin Situ: Pretty much everything about the transaction. The venue, time, schedule. Nin Situ: He didn't tell everything outright, but gave us enough leads. <<#Simple(8)>> ->So you suspected it's all planned. Nin Situ: Yes.;<> <<#GoTo(@跳過司徒懷疑是誘餌對話)>> ->Isn't that a good thing? Nin Situ: My field experience tells me to never trust anything too good to be true. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I had reasons to believe Chameleon wanted us to be there at that time. <<@跳過司徒懷疑是誘餌對話>> Nin Situ: He went out of his way to lay this elaborate trap, because it was against his boss' orders. Nin Situ: This could sabotage the deal, but he just couldn't let go of this chance to finally kill me. Nin Situ: So he did it behind Vision's back. Frances Cheng: But why did you keep this knowledge from us? Nin Situ: Would you walk into a trap knowing it's a trap?;<> Nin Situ: Especially 23. I don't have a chance without her support. Nin Situ: And I can't let those children fall into Vision's hands.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->And you also can't let go of this chance to finally kill Chameleon. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Yes.;<> Thought: I thought she'd deny. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: The plan was to evacuate the children and get out before their main force arrive. Nin Situ: If we run into Chameleon, I'll keep him occupied while 23 or Helper get the children. Nin Situ: The events were more or less within the plan, except that...;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Except what? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Chameleon brought way too many men for reinforcement. Nin Situ: No, that's not right... Nin Situ: I wasn't expecting him to try so hard just to kill me.;<> Nin Situ: Anyway, there's no conspiracy. I only kept a relatively unimportant portion of the truth from you. <<#GoTo(@第四日問司徒寧循環)>> ->Why didn't he use the children as hostages? ($DLStateFloat2);//選過消失 <>;<> Nin Situ: He knew it wouldn't work. Nin Situ: If he pushed me into that corner, things could turn extremely ugly. I'd sacrifice the children. Nin Situ: I wouldn't allow myself to be killed. That is my bottom line. Thought: 23 is right about Situ...;<> Nin Situ: Compared to the children, I am more valuable to the world. Thought: She said that without a moment of hesitation...;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->You're heartless. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: You think I'm heartless because I didn't hesitate. Nin Situ: To you, what I said sounds arrogant, self-righteous. Nin Situ: It is not true. ->You were willing to see children injured or killed. Nin Situ: Yes. ->What if any of them grows into an inventor who changes the world? Nin Situ: They are from poor families in the Shithole. Nin Situ: No citizenship, no social security, no education. Nin Situ: Objectively, the chance of them growing into anything but flop contributors is next to nil. <<#Simple(12)>> ->And that makes them expendable? They should be sacrificed? Nin Situ: Could, not should. Nin Situ: Given the right condition, anyone is expendable.;<> Nin Situ: The condition doesn't justify the price. Death is never to be celebrated.;<> Nin Situ: However... Nin Situ: Death can be acceptable. Nin Situ: ...;<>;<> Nin Situ: I hope you could understand. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I make quick decisions, not perfect decisions. This line of work calls for it. Nin Situ: It's impossible to literally take into consideration every single factor.;<> Thought: She looks kind of...ashamed? <<#Simple(8)>> ->I know what you mean. Nin Situ: Thank you.;<> Thought: She's somewhat grateful. ->But how do you calculate the worth of people? Nin Situ: I have my own standards.;<> Nin Situ: Anyone could argue against them. Nin Situ: And I may revise them from time to time, too. Nin Situ: But at the end of the day, everything in this world must have a worth. Nin Situ: I calculate this worth based on my own standards, not social conventions. Nin Situ: I make my own decisions, from scratch. ->...;<> <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@第四日問司徒寧循環)>> ->Why did you send me in instead of Lawrence? ($DLStateFloat3);//選過消失 <>;<> Nin Situ: Tactical planning. Nin Situ: Our van was the only means of evacuation if things went south. Also, 23 was running the mobile command center in it.;<> Nin Situ: I need an experienced team member to protect it. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see. // ->Couldn't 23 protect herself and the van? Nin Situ: She could, but she's also running our command center. Nin Situ: Couldn't afford to have her distracted. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: But again, honestly... Nin Situ: That was the main reason, not the whole reason. Nin Situ: I also hoped to keep Lawrence out of harm's way.;<>;<> Nin Situ: He's not ready to face risks of this level. Nin Situ: With Chameleon and his mercenary group, the situation was a miniature warfare. Nin Situ: I made a mistake at the Flop Core. The same mistake can't happen again. Frances Cheng: But Jason isn't ready to face this, either!;<>;<> Nin Situ: No. You forgot he's like me. We are not entirely human. Nin Situ: We are damn hard to kill, and stand up much faster after taking a blow. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You think I could afford more risk? Nin Situ: Yes.;<> Thought: Well, at least she's honest. ->Damn hard to kill, not unkillable. Nin Situ: Right. A bullet in the head does the job.;<> Nin Situ: Otherwise we can soak quite a bit of damage without suffering permanent injury. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Does that justify dropping Jason in the middle of a war zone?;<> Nin Situ: I don't mean that. Nin Situ: It was all tactical planning. Risks and consequences.;<> Nin Situ: Lawrence's role for the van, Jason's survival advantage, I considered everything. Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: I see. Let's leave it at that, for now. Nin Situ: Good.;<> <> <<#GoTo(@第四日問司徒寧循環)>> ->Helper told me you work for a corporation. ($DLStateFloat4);//選過消失 <>;<> <>;//有問起這件事 Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Before he left? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You're not answering my questions. ->Yes. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: He's not giving you the whole truth. <<#Simple(8)>> ->And you didn't give me any truth at all. Nin Situ: ...I won't argue.;<> Nin Situ: Please let me explain.;<> ->So you are working for a corporation. Nin Situ: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: This involves classified information.;<> Nin Situ: If you insist, I will try my best to tell you what I can disclose. Nin Situ: But for you own good, I'd say please don't ask.;<> Frances Cheng: Why? Nin Situ: Because what little knowledge I can give will lead to even more questions. You will be worse off. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Got to say, that's fishy like hell. Nin Situ: You're right. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: If you want peace of mind, you won't get it. Trust me. Nin Situ: This is why I kept it from you this whole time. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't dodge my question. Nin Situ: I'm willing to provide answers. It's just that you must think twice. Thought: She looks dead serious. I guess she's not lying. ->What exactly is going on? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Without disclosing any more, I promise that I will not compromise your interest. Nin Situ: From here on, it's up to you. Do you wish to pursue this subject? Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: What do you say, J? Thought: I must get to the bottom of it... Thought: Whatever Situ is hiding, we have the right to know.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Tell me. Nin Situ: What about Frances... Frances Cheng: I want the truth, too. <<@司徒說明公司真相第一階>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: You asked for it, here it is.;<> Nin Situ: It's true that every OWL agent is assigned to a corporation. I'm no exception. Nin Situ: But I do not follow the orders of my theoretical boss. <<#Simple(8)>> ->That doesn't make sense. ->Whose orders do you follow, then? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: That is as much as I'm allowed to disclose. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What? That's it? You barely started! Nin Situ: Yes. That's it. ->Why did they bother assigning you to this theoretical boss, then? Nin Situ: That is classified. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Ms. Situ, what— Nin Situ: Sorry, I have said everything that can be said. Nin Situ: As you were warned, now you have more questions than answers. Nin Situ: From here on, knowledge will come at a high price. <<#Simple(6)>> ->What kind of price? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: I trust you can keep a secret. But if word gets out that you have learned about it... Nin Situ: You will become public enemies. A pair of fugitives hunted down by the government and all corporations.;<> Nin Situ: All my colleagues at OWL will be sent after you, and the order will be extermination on sight. Thought: That look... She's not exaggerating.;<> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: But— Frances Cheng: That is an unlikely if, am I right? Nin Situ: ...Yes. Frances Cheng: Then I want the whole truth.;<> Frances Cheng: What about you, J? Thought: She's determined.;<> Thought: Chameleon said I should start to make my own moves. Thought: Was he referring to this moment? <> <>;//對司徒真實背景的理解為「有隸屬某間公司，但不聽從他們」 <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I want the whole truth. <>;//對司徒真實背景的理解為「知道私兵的事情」 Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: Good. Curiosity kills the cat. Nin Situ: Keep two things in mind, though.;<> Nin Situ: One. I'm only doing this to earn your trust, which is critical for the mission tomorrow. Nin Situ: Two. You have made an informed decision, fully acknowledging all possible risks that I have truthfully disclosed. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Noted and acknowledged. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I'm well aware. Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: I'm in fact a free agent serving Grandma only. Nin Situ: I do have a corporate sponsor, but it's only on paper. This is kept secret even from other OWL agents. Thought: I've heard about this Grandma before...;<> Frances Cheng: Grandma is the leader of OWL, I think? Nin Situ: Correct.;<> Frances Cheng: ...What's the risk? It sounds safe enough.;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: The corporations collectively control the government, and OWL is their shared tool.;<> Nin Situ: It's a powerful weapon, also a vast spy network. Nin Situ: To make sure OWL doesn't work in any particular corporation's favor, they need a neutral force to manage it. Nin Situ: This neutral force is Grandma. Nin Situ: Despite every countermeasure, the corporations have one fundamental concern.;<> Frances Cheng: That Grandma may have her own free will.;<> Nin Situ: Indeed.;<> Nin Situ: Each of them have a share in OWL, but at the end of the day, it's Grandma who gives orders. Frances Cheng: I think I know where this is going... <<#Simple(6)>> ->You lost me. What does that insinuate? Nin Situ: Simply put, Grandma is strictly forbidden to have her own agents. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: If they learn about me, heads will roll. Grandma and everyone involved, all wiped from this world. Nin Situ: That includes me, and you two. Frances Cheng: What if Grandma learns about this conversation...?;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: Honestly, I don't know. Nin Situ: I have assessed all possible outcomes... Nin Situ: In most of them, you will be silenced.;<> Frances Cheng: ...;<>;<> Nin Situ: I can't just say I don't know, because you're too smart to buy it. Nin Situ: Although I really don't think you're better off with this knowledge.;<> Nin Situ: Now you know why. Nin Situ: I'm sorry, for everything.;<> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Nin Situ: Knowing more is not necessarily a good thing. Nin Situ: Especially in this trade.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Most would follow orders and stay within boundaries, for survival.;<> Thought: Boundaries, huh... Nin Situ: Do you have any questions? <> <> <> <<@問司徒寧關於私兵時間>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Does 23 know? ($DLStateInt);//選過消失 <> Nin Situ: Yes, but she's not a free agent herself.;<> Nin Situ: She found out on her own. 23 is a smart woman. Thought: She seems rather proud of 23. ($DLStateFloat);//選過消失 <> Nin Situ: Not possible. Nin Situ: You may get lucky a couple of times. Trust me. <<#GoTo(@補問主角被鴞隊獵殺的情況)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: So.;<> Nin Situ: Here comes more bad news. Nin Situ: OWL might find out what you are sooner than I expected. Nin Situ: That means, if you do want to join OWL, you must hurry up. Frances Cheng: ...Why?;<> Nin Situ: As I said, Chameleon is now in the custody of OWL. Nin Situ: Grandma is now aware of our operation. She's paying close attention, at least she will, from tonight on. Nin Situ: In the process, she may very likely find out what you are. Frances Cheng: ...Wait, she is aware of our operation?;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Meaning we must stop? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: No. Nin Situ: If she wanted to stop, we should all have been apprehended by now. Nin Situ: Since it didn't happen, there are two possibilities.;<> Nin Situ: One. Grandma knows what we're doing but not why we're doing it. Nin Situ: It will take her at least one day to find out. Our mission tomorrow is not affected. Nin Situ: Two. She knew everything all along, but chose not to intervene... Nin Situ: That means, for whatever reason, I have her consent.;<> Nin Situ: Maybe she wants to teach Vision a lesson? Nin Situ: Or maybe a part of my operation happens to be aligned with her own scheme, whatever it is? Nin Situ: I truly don't know. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Maybe she cares about social justice like you do. Nin Situ: Impossible.;<> Nin Situ: There's no such thing as justice in her eyes. Thought: She sounds like she loathes Grandma. Nin Situ: Anyway.;<> ->How much time do we have? Nin Situ: I'm not sure, but... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: At least for the next week, our operation will be safe enough. Nin Situ: After that? Can't really say. Frances Cheng: ... Nin Situ: Now, Jason, we must prepare you for your big day. Nin Situ: Training starts as soon as we wrap up the mission tomorrow. Thought: What if this training doesn't go well? Will OWL kill me?;<> Nin Situ: Don't worry about it, Jason. Nin Situ: I don't know how you did it, but as far as I've noticed... Nin Situ: Your potential has been flexing itself for whatever reason I don't understand.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Shall I take it as good news? Nin Situ: You should.;<> <<#GoTo(@司徒解釋主角能力上升)>> ->Reasons you don't understand? <<@司徒解釋主角能力上升>> Nin Situ: Your potential was showing when I first saw you, which is normal. Nin Situ: The thing is, it keeps showing more, even now. Nin Situ: That is unexpected. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Your training will go very well. I have no doubt about that. Frances Cheng: What's the exact scope of his training? Nin Situ: Starting from the integration of awarenesses.;<> Thought: Integration of what? Nin Situ: The reason why you can't always operate in full power mode is that your mind is fractured. Nin Situ: Your persona exists in two parts. One of them is reserved for possession. Nin Situ: You must break down the walls sealing this part off to gain full control of your body.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->How could I do that? // ->I'm not possessed. Do I still need to integrate these awarenesses? Nin Situ: Pardon my phrasing. "Awarenesses" may not be the right word. Nin Situ: In fact, you're dealing with two parts of the same awareness. Nin Situ: You must acknowledge the existence of the walled up part and gain access to it. Nin Situ: For that... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: You have already been doing it. Nin Situ: First of all, notice that your memory is not complete. There are things that you do not know you know. Nin Situ: You did it when you were informed of your nature. Thought: My nature... Weaponized human.;<> //glitch開始微弱出現 Nin Situ: With this breakthrough, the separation in your mind will start to weaken, eventually crumble. Nin Situ: In the process, you may have hallucinations, most likely of a place that doesn't exist.;<> Frances Cheng: Place that doesn't exist?;<> Nin Situ: Figment of imagination. It could be a bedroom, a pasture, a shower stall, anything.;<> Nin Situ: Whatever it looks like, this is where the dominant side of your persona goes during possession.;<> Nin Situ: Whenever the invading awareness barges in, you are shoved into this little corner. Nin Situ: Once possession ends, you're free to leave that corner. Nin Situ: But all memories created during possession are left there.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I need to, what, retrieve this archive of memories? Nin Situ: Yes.;<> ->Huh...? Nin Situ: Sorry if I'm not doing a good job explaining it. Nin Situ: It's a lot to process. Nin Situ: Here's a simplified version... Nin Situ: All memories created during possession are locked up in an imagined space within your mind. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Complete your memory. Gain full control of your body. Nin Situ: Without being possessed, you must find a way to get into this space, and find your way out of it. Frances Cheng: The way out?;<> Nin Situ: There is always a way out, often materialized as a locked window or door. Nin Situ: However, it takes a tremendous amount of willpower to open up this way.;<> Nin Situ: That would be the objective of your training. Nin Situ: We will cover the specifics when you go consciously into this space for the first time. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Can't we talk about it now? Nin Situ: Right now is not the time. Nin Situ: We can't rush this process. Sorry. ->I think I've already been there. Frances Cheng: Huh? J? Really?;<>;<> Nin Situ: It could happen, since he is already aware of his nature. Thought: I went before I even knew it. Nin Situ: What is your space like?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It looks like my hometown. ->Exactly like the bedroom in our apartment. ->It's the hospital room where my granny died. Nin Situ: You mean it resembles an actual place?;<> Nin Situ: Hmm, that is new to me. Nin Situ: ...;<> ->It's like an interrogation room. I'm locked inside. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I see.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I need to run a few medical tests to verify.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Let's leave this for later, when we wrap up this Vision business. Nin Situ: I said we should hurry, but honestly, it's only a suspicion. Nin Situ: Let's prepare for the worst case scenario. Nin Situ: Anyway, now you know what you need to know. Frances Cheng: Yeah...;<> Nin Situ: This is a lot to process. Take your time.;<>;<> <<#End>>

title: 麵攤4
body: //大家安靜吃一下東西 23: ... 23: Damn, you guys are boring. Lawrence Chang: Haha...;<> 23: Show some spirit! Tomorrow is the big day! 23: Waiter! Beer!;<>;<> <>;<> Ivan Zhang: This is a family-friendly noodle house.;<> 23: Do you have beer or not? Ivan Zhang: ...If you have money. 23: I'm filthy rich! Drown me with beer!;<>;<> Ivan Zhang: Public servants these days... Ivan Zhang: ...;<>;<>;<>;<>;//看一下螢幕 Ivan Zhang: I need to go out for a while. Wait for the robot waiter to bring your beer. 23: Who's gonna watch your joint?;<> Ivan Zhang: With you people here, I don't need to worry about thieves.;<> 23: Right! Haha!!!;<> Ivan Zhang: Tsk.;<> <> Lawrence Chang: Ugh, I think I'll pass on the beer. Can't have a hangover tomorrow...;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I'll pass, too. ->I don't mind a hangover. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Huh?;<> 23: Did I order for you? 23: The beer is all mine. Lawrence Chang: You can't hold a beer.;<>;<> 23: I know. 23: Nah, enough nagging.;<> 23: Let's talk about something fun. Lawrence Chang: Like what? 23: I don't know. Jason, you start. <> <> <> <> <> <> <<@第四日跟23隆生聊天問題環節>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->How did it go with those kids? ($DLStateFloat) <>;<> 23: Come on! No work talk!;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->This is kinda important. 23: The point of having fun is to not talk about important stuff!;<> ->I'm curious. <> ->Sorry. 23: If I got one penny for every sorry you said, I'd have... 23: ...I'd have how many pennies again?;<> <<#EndSimple>> 23: Anyway! 23: Just know that they're safe, OK?;<> 23: I have a friend in the Shithole and he gets stuff done, alright? Lawrence Chang: I heard it's an awesome hacker, helping people out all the time.;<>;<>;<> 23: She's awesome, but I'm awesomer. 23: She's gonna return those brats to their own homes. Leave it to her.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Got it. // ->Won't Vision come after them again? 23: Her people will keep watching for a while.;<> 23: For the next week or two, we will know if anything happens. 23: Again, Vision just needs kids, not these specific kids.;<> 23: It's a lot easier to buy a new batch. 23: But I guess Vision will lay low for a while.;<> 23: They want to avoid attention. Keep pushing their luck and the police will notice. 23: Too much trouble for its worth. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Are you the police? 23: I mean the regular kind of police, not OWL.;<> 23: Vision can buy them, but it takes money. 23: The easiest and cheapest way is to lay low and wait it out.;<> ->I see. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: The gang they hired won't make a move, because they are not paid yet. 23: That summarizes the situation. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Now, talk about something else. <> <<#GoTo(@第四日跟23隆生聊天問題環節)>> ->How is it going between you and Situ? ($DLStateFloat2) <>;<> 23: What do you think? It's been only a day or two since you last checked!;<> 23: And we're busy working on the case this whole time! 23: I thought by now it should have been over. Well, look what we have.;<> 23: *Sigh* <<#Simple(8)>> ->By this time tomorrow it has to be over, for real. 23: ... 23: Yeah, I agree. ->Isn't that like the norm of your life? 23: Ha, the norm it is. 23: One case after another, never any private time... ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: The thing is, given the scope and scale of this mess...;<> 23: I don't think it's ever going to be "over". There will be one hell of an aftermath. 23: Hey why are we talking about work again! 23: Bad boy, Jason!;<> Lawrence Chang: He asked about your relationship. Then you led it to work.;<> 23: Alright! My bad!;<> 23: Let's try again.;<> <> <<#GoTo(@第四日跟23隆生聊天問題環節)>> ->How did you get to know Ivan Zhang? ($DLStateFloat3) <>;<> <> 23: Old man Zhang?;<> 23: Heh. Long story.;<> 23: Let's start from the beginning. 23: I said it's a long story, mind you. You asked for it.;<> 23: He's an outsider, originally from Huayen... 23: Wait. These days it's called District 13.;<> 23: Back in his hometown he was some kind of high roller, owned a mining company. Lawrence Chang: Wow. 23: All was good until one day Growell wanted to take over his business. Then, you know.;<> 23: Growell was on an expansion spree in Huayen, buying every single competitor out. 23: For those who did not want to sell, they had plenty of ways to persuade them. 23: So, old man Zhang sold his mine to Growell on the cheap, before anything could happen to his family.;<> Lawrence Chang: There's a big gap between business owner in Huayen and weapon smuggler in Wanpei?;<>;<> 23: I'm coming to that part! Shut up!;<> Lawrence Chang: Okay.;<> 23: Now, Zhang isn't a man to just quietly go away.;<> 23: He moved his family out, pulled some strings, and got a shitload of dynamites... Lawrence Chang: No shit... 23: When Growell came to check their new purchase, the mine was wiped off the map. Lawrence Chang: That's what you'd call going out with a bang!;<> 23: It's nothing. Here comes the fun part. Growell was pissed. They tore the local mining community apart to find the culprit.;<> 23: With Zhang making an example, local miners and business owners united to duke it out with Growell.;<> 23: The riot went on for four months. Gunfights pretty much every day. Dead bodies everywhere. 23: Basically a civil war.;<> Lawrence Chang: Hold on. How come I've never heard about it? Thought: Me, either...;<> Thought: All I can remember about Growell and Huayen are positive stories. Lawrence Chang: Ah, I just recalled some story about a protest or something.;<> Lawrence Chang: But a civil war? Seriously?;<> 23: Always trust the government's censorship capability.;<> Lawrence Chang: How did it end? 23: The locals were no match for Growell in terms of firepower. 23: No worries! Our old man Zhang came to help. He pulled a couple of strings at Huayen Airport.;<> Lawrence Chang: How many strings does this guy have? 23: Anyway, he got a portion of Sunrise's export weapons before they could be shipped out.;<> Lawrence Chang: You're shitting me...;<> 23: With these supplies, the war escalated. The local government couldn't do a thing. 23: After a while, the central government decided enough is enough. They asked Sunrise to step in.;<>;<> 23: No surprise. You know which side won. Lawrence Chang: How many died? 23: Nine. Lawrence Chang: Just nine?;<> 23: Huh? Just nine?;<> Lawrence Chang: You know what I mean. With Growell and Sunrise together, it should have been a massacre. 23: Sunrise just sent two OWL agents.;<> 23: They picked out all riot leaders within a day.;<> 23: Their families, friends, associates, all disposed of. 23: Then the riot was over. 23: You know the rest. Growell controlled Huayen's mining business.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> 23: As to Zhang...;<> 23: He started the war, then fled to Wanpei under a new identity. 23: Here he built up a little smuggling empire, using the profit to support victims of the Huayen civil war.;<> 23: That includes both riot participants and those involuntarily involved.;<> 23: Sneaking them out of Huayen, giving cash support, so on and so forth. Lawrence Chang: ...So he's a good man. 23: I wouldn't call him that.;<> 23: ... Lawrence Chang: So...;<> Lawrence Chang: How did you get to know him? 23: ...I was assigned to handle the aftermath of the riot.;<> 23: In the process I found Zhang and his current business. 23: Instead of turning him in, I offered a partnership. 23: ... <<#Simple(8)>> ->OWL doesn't know about him? 23: Theoretically. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Theoretically? 23: I didn't turn him in, and the case was closed.;<> 23: No one should know about him. 23: Or if anyone did, they chose to let him go. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Why didn't you turn him in? 23: ... 23: Guess?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He's a valuable ally. ->You think he's a good man. ->You want him to keep helping people. <<#EndSimple>> 23: You think I'm that kind, huh.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Answer me. 23: Sorry, I don't feel like telling. 23: Let's just say your guess is the correct answer. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: ... 23: Phew, that was one long story.;<> 23: Now you know Zhang's background. 23: Don't tell anyone. Mind you.;<> Lawrence Chang: Of course I won't. 23: Now, let's try something else. <> <<#GoTo(@第四日跟23隆生聊天問題環節)>> ->What kind of person is Chameleon? ($DLStateFloat4) <>;<> 23: Scum.;<> 23: No background. No history. Even OWL can't find anything on him. 23: He takes money from big corps and do their dirty work. Like this time. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Can't OWL take him out? 23: Sure we can. 23: But his little business doesn't justify the cost.;<> 23: Most importantly... 23: Since we are the law enforcement, there are things we cannot publicly do.;<> 23: Hence there's a real need for their services in the market. ->He only works for corps? Why? 23: Because mercenary groups, like everyone else, prefer reliable clients.;<> 23: Being an illegal trade, they can't sue the employer for breach of contract. 23: Dealing with the police is their number one taboo. Haha.;<> 23: Corps have a reputation to protect. If things go south, mercenary groups could blackmail them. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Speaking of Chameleon...;<> 23: He's a quirky case even in the world of mercenaries. Very unpredictable. 23: Hiring him means there's always a chance of backfiring. 23: He's still in business, because his men are the best.;<> 23: ... 23: I'm surprised that Vision hired him. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why? 23: Vision needs someone to get things done, like the transaction today. 23: They should have hired someone more reliable.;<> 23: So I guess today was only a side job. Chameleon was really hired for the security of the facility. Lawrence Chang: What facility?;<> 23: What else? The one we're about to break into tomorrow!;<> Lawrence Chang: Ah yes! 23: Again, Chameleon's group isn't known for security business. This is fishy.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What are they known for, then? 23: Demolition. 23: Massacre. 23: Stuff like that. 23: Loud, messy jobs. ->Yeah, fishy. 23: Right? ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Also, it doesn't make sense that Chameleon took the contract.;<> 23: He and his men enjoy action. They are psychopaths. 23: Things like armed escort and security guard are considered boring.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What does that mean? 23: I have no idea. ->How do you know them that well? 23: Personal experience. 23: He has been wreaking havoc everywhere for a long while. There's a clear pattern. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: There must be something we don't know going on, but we don't have time to find out what. 23: Whatever it is, treat Chameleon as a major threat. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: He's wanted by OWL, because he kills OWL agents for the trill of it. 23: He has taken out, how many? Four? Yeah, four of us.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...What?;<> Lawrence Chang: Is he that dangerous?;<> 23: Nin engaged him quite a few times. She had the upper hand, but couldn't apprehend or kill him.;<>;<> 23: ... 23: Chameleon, on the other hand, wants to kill Nin at all costs. Today is just another example.;<>;<> //IF有先說 <<#If($ToldCanRetreat=0)>> 23: And Nin walked right into his trap... 23: Thank you, Jason. Or else I would know.;<> Thought: ... 23: ...;<> <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why does he hate Situ so much? 23: ... 23: For him, Nin is a major pain in the ass. 23: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Let's talk about something else, Jason?;<> <> <<#GoTo(@第四日跟23隆生聊天問題環節)>> ->I'm running out of subjects. ($DLStateFloat5) <> <<#EndSimple>> 23: Okay. 23: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: 23...;<> Lawrence Chang: Aren't you drinking too much? Don't forget tomorrow— 23: Exactly! Drinking is part of my preparation ritual.;<> Lawrence Chang: No such thing!;<> 23: ...;<> <<#End>>

title: 麵攤5
body: //等一下，也許攝影機對一下電視機感覺主角再看？ 23: Hey, listen.;<>;//攝影機拉回來 23: Suppose you love this person, you'd want them happy, right? Lawrence Chang: Is that even a question? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Of course. I'd want Fran happy. // ->That's one wild change of subjects. 23: I said no work talk, alright? ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: ... 23: What if this person keeps doing stuff that makes themselves unhappy? What will you do?;<> Lawrence Chang: Huh? Why would they do that?;<>;<> 23: They just do! Maybe psychological disorder or something. You get the idea. Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: I'll probably help them get over this disorder, maybe...? Lawrence Chang: Got to do something. Can't just leave them be. 23: What about you, Jason?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Find something I can do for them. <<@也是主動看能做點什麼吧>> 23: Right? Normal people think like that.;<> ->I wouldn't do a thing unless they come to me asking for help. <<@我不會主動做什麼，除非他向我求助>> 23: You just watch them sink deeper into depression?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. I'd do something when they are...maybe waist deep in it. 23: What if they push you back and say they don't want no help?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I guess I'll administer help by force. 23: So it depends on the specific situation?;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->More or less. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Then I'd respect their decision. 23: Really...?;<> <<#EndSimple>> ->I don't want to watch, but I want to mess with people's lives even less. 23: Really...? <<#EndSimple>> ->I don't know. Every person is a different case. 23: True. But there should be a general principle? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Okay. Maybe I'll do something for them. <<#GoTo(@也是主動看能做點什麼吧)>> ->I wouldn't do a thing unless they come to me asking for help. <<#GoTo(@我不會主動做什麼，除非他向我求助)>> ->No. I judge these things case by case. 23: OK.;<> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Is this somehow related to Nin? 23: ...;<> 23: Yeah. 23: She's kinda self-abusive, won't you say so? <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I agree. Thought: 23 gave me a smile that seems to mean "I know right?" ->What do you mean by "self-abusive"? 23: She keeps stepping into businesses that have totally nothing to do with her. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah, she's like that. Thought: 23 gave me a smile that seems to mean "I know right?" ->I don't think so. 23: ... 23: No. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Nin has this sense of responsibility. Everybody has it, but hers is pathologically strong. 23: She just has to do something for the world, to save, to correct. <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->With great power comes great responsibility. ($DLStateFloat);//不管怎樣都會跳的姊所選項 23: Ha! Yes!;<> 23: She quotes that all the time. 23: It sounds good, but doesn't make sense.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->How so? 23: Because it's an unreasonable order. 23: Power and responsibility are separate things.;<> 23: Whoever came up with this line is telling people with power to take up more responsibility. 23: It's not a proposal, it gives no reason, just a blunt order. 23: I mean, who the hell takes up additional responsibility for no reason... 23: Just because a random dude said so?;<> 23: Nin did just that, knowing that she doesn't have to. <<#GoTo(@跳過寧選擇遵從這句)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: But Nin follows it to at T... ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@跳過寧選擇遵從這句>> Lawrence Chang: But...;<> Lawrence Chang: Is it a bad thing?;<> Lawrence Chang: I mean, at least I look up to Nin because she does that. 23: ...;<>;<> 23: I'm just...;<> 23: I'm worried about her. 23: This sense of responsibility makes her stubborn. 23: She does things with terrible risks because of it. 23: All these secret investigations without OWL's approval. 23: The risk and scale escalates every time... 23: This time against Vision, we're screwing with something way too big for us, and we don't even know if there's a legit case. 23: I've been against this operation from the beginning, but she just...;<> 23: ... 23: She just won't listen.;<> 23: ...;<> 23: Involving civilians, invading a flop farm without a warrant. 23: Breaking into the Flop Core. 23: We are not even sure if there is anything to investigate, and she's broken more laws than I can count. Lawrence Chang: ...;<>;//隆生在這裡意識到司徒寧可能是蔓生派去的人 23: All this time, I'm just standing by her side, being her minion. //if主角有提 <<#If($ToldCanRetreat=0)>> 23: ... 23: Today is just another example.;<> 23: She could have pulled out, but chose to bet with her own and Jason's lives. 23: Thank you for telling me, Jason. Or else I wouldn't know. 23: She'd never tell me.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->She was going to tell you. 23: Sure, but not today, right? 23: Wait till this is over, she'd say. When the timing is right. And the timing is never right. Thought: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> 23: ... 23: "It's the only way." She uses the same excuse every single time.;<> 23: Well, there are other ways, she just chooses to not see them. 23: I'm... 23: *Sigh*;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: She does what has to be done.;<> 23: ...Huh?;<> Lawrence Chang: Ugh, nothing.;<> 23: ... 23: Huh, she taught you well.;<> 23: Now we have a pair of consequentialists.;<> Lawrence Chang: Consequ-what? 23: The kind of people who believes the consequence justifies the means. If the result is good, the process doesn't matter.;<> 23: Isn't that how you see things?;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Probably. 23: I'm not like you.;<> Thought: She seems to be tired of this conversation already. 23: Me and Nin, we're not the same type.;<> 23: ... Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: You know what, 23? Maybe we are both piling too much expectation on Nin.;<> 23: What do you mean?;<> Lawrence Chang: I know I idolize her, and I'm not ashamed of it.;<> Lawrence Chang: The Nin I see is not real. It's what I want her to be. Thought: Lawrence is somewhat dejected? 23: ... 23: I'm not like you. Idolizing is not my thing.;<> Lawrence Chang: Right, you're not idolizing her. Lawrence Chang: But we're doing essentially the same thing. Lawrence Chang: You want Nin to grow into what you want her to be, but she refuses to.;<> Lawrence Chang: She's just the person that she is. You are projecting unrealistic expectations onto her.;<> Lawrence Chang: In the process, you forget that she's her own woman. Lawrence Chang: Instead of trying to understand her... Lawrence Chang: You choose to enforce demands on her.;<> Lawrence Chang: Come to think about it... Lawrence Chang: Isn't that kind of...selfish? 23: ... 23: ...;<>;<> 23: Ha... 23: Dude, are you really Lawrence Chang? Not possessed or high on something? 23: I can't believe you just said that load of therapist crap. Lawrence Chang: ...;<>;<> Thought: Lawrence can't look her in the eye. 23: ... 23: You know what? You're right.;<> 23: I wanted Nin to grow into the woman I want, since day one.;<> 23: I never accepted her as she is. 23: A lot of her decisions seem ridiculous to me.;<> 23: I call her stubborn, unreasonable. The truth is... 23: ...;<> 23: I'm just too selfish to even try to understand her. <<#Simple(8)>> ->It happens to everyone. Love blinds. 23: Yeah, I guess I'm normal? ->Does the sudden realization change anything? 23: Nope. 23: Nothing at all. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> 23: ... 23: I still love her. //23喝一大口酒 <> 23: Okay, I'm done.;//23撐桌站起 Lawrence Chang: ...So soon? 23: Gotta prepare for tomorrow and dress Nin's wounds.;<>;<>;<> //23淡出到桌子側邊 Lawrence Chang: Just dress the wounds. No hanky-panky. 23: I'm a woman with class!;<>;//摸摸後頸 Thought: She looks really, really tired. 23: Good night, you two.;<> Lawrence Chang: Night. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Good night. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //23解除摸後頸，淡出到街景走掉 <<#End>>

title: 麵攤6
body:

<<#EndSimple>>

title: 23大接露
body: <> 23: Haha... 23: That didn't go well... Thought: What the hell is going on!? Nin Situ: You're shot by your own tranquilizer. Nin Situ: Stop fighting. 23: Sure. I'm not stupid. 23: Heh...;<> 23: I thought a had a chance against you... 23: Now I see why they say you're inhuman, haha...;<> Nin Situ: Do you think this is fun? Thought: Situ looks angry and bewildered at the same time. Thought: Two emotions that I'd never expect to see on her face. Nin Situ: What the hell do you think you're doing!?;<> Nin Situ: What is your problem!? 23: Hey, calm... 23: Calm down. 23: You never lose your cool, Nin. Nin Situ: I never...!? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: ...;<> //司徒站好喘了口氣 Nin Situ: Jason Dai.;//司徒轉過身或投看相主角 <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes. ->What's going on? <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: 23 tried to knock me out with tranquilizer. 23: Tried and failed. 23: Do you sleep with your eyes open or what? Nin Situ: I knew you'd do something. 23: ...You knew? Nin Situ: I knew you're the snitch. Thought: What? We really do have a snitch among us!? 23: When? Nin Situ: When watching Frances's holographic recording. Nin Situ: The moment that Chameleon appeared... 23: I was visibly shaken. Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: Yes. 23: I was shocked to see him in this case, really. Nin Situ: Vision is always half a step ahead of us since this operation started. I thought it was just bad luck. Nin Situ: But after that moment, I started to suspect you. First I did some tests. 23: Ah, the ID card for Lawrence the other day... 23: You changed the title mid-operation, to see when the information was leaked. 23: Haha, textbook trick.;<> Nin Situ: But you fell for it.;<> 23: ... 23: Yep, I did. 23: Because I never for once thought you'd suspect me.;<> Nin Situ: Ironic. 23: ...Yeah. Nin Situ: The bug discovered in Frances's wound was fabricated. There was no such thing. Nin Situ: Vision could have done a lot worse if they did plant a bug. Nin Situ: We could have been dead by now. 23: Yes, I only provided them with a tiny little bit of info. 23: Enough to hinder your progress, but not to cause us physical danger. Nin Situ: And you attempt to turn Lawrence against me... That was like a joke. 23: Haha... 23: I was counting on him to at least slow you down with his tantrums. 23: Now we know it didn't work at all. Nin Situ: Because I trust you and Lawrence. Nin Situ: Even if I know you're a snitch. Nin Situ: Even if I have no idea why you did that. Nin Situ: I fully trust that you won't harm me.;<> 23: ... 23: Come on, don't talk like that.;<> 23: It makes me feel like a pathetic piece of shit, you know? Nin Situ: ... 23: ...;//23低頭不看司徒 23: Do you hate me, Nin? 23: I won't be surprised if you do. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: 23 Nin Situ: Why? 23: Because I love you.;<>;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: This is not the time... 23: I mean it, Nin. 23: I have to stop you before it's too late. 23: There's something terrible at the end of this path. It will hurt you. 23: I'm doing everything I can to delay that moment. 23: I just want to protect you, Nin. Nin Situ: Are you delusional or what? Nin Situ: Protect me? By endangering my partners?;<> Nin Situ: They're your partners, too! 23: Sacrificing partners is a part of our job. Nin Situ: What? No! I— 23: Where's your ex comms officer? Enjoying retirement on a tropical island, huh? Nin Situ: I didn't mean to— 23: You are the one partner I can't afford to lose. 23: ... 23: I don't have the heart to see you hurt. Nin Situ: Hurt? I'm not your average hu—;<> 23: I don't mean physical injury. 23: The way you do things, physical injury is inevitable. 23: You won't get to die in your own bed and I have no problem with it. 23: I'm worried about things that could mentally break you... Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Mentally break me? You almost did it! Nin Situ: Think about it! What you did to Lawrence! Nin Situ: You could have killed him!;<> 23: Between he and Jason, they could handle the situation. Getting out was not a problem.;<> Nin Situ: You think I'm talking about the Flop Core? 23: ...;<> Nin Situ: He trusted you!;<> Nin Situ: He trusted you more than anyone else, more than me! Do you know that? Nin Situ: Do you have any idea how much you mean to him? Nin Situ: He's relying on you, 23. 23: ... 23: I know what I did. I don't deserve him... //23縮起來把頭埋起來之類的 <<#End>>

title: 第四日內心世界
body:
//幫手說的對司徒的評語，主角怎麼看。
//對司徒的信任。

//司徒寧也提到過的吧？門就是出口//我是過了完全打不開，但今天門把可以轉了，也許正在鬆動。

//思考老張給的退路，我們已經被鴞隊盯上了，到頭來司徒寧也只是在幫鴞隊做事。



Jason Dai: ...Hey.
<<#Simple(6)>>
->Hey.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Jason Dai: So, what do you think?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Huh?
->About what? It's been one hell of a long day. Which part?
    Jason Dai: Situ.
->I think her exit plan is quite good.
    Jason Dai: Yes.
    Jason Dai: Promise Vision to keep their secret, and bargain for our freedom...
    Jason Dai: Yeah, sounds like a plan.
    Jason Dai: What about herself?
<<#EndSimple>>

<<#Simple(8)>>
->You mean what Chameleon and Helper said?
->That Situ could be working for a competitor of Vision's?
    Jason Dai: ...Correct.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Jason Dai: Is she hiding something from us?
//if有問到底
<<#If($SiTuRealMaster=2)>>
    Jason Dai: I know you pursued the subject, and she did give an answer.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Do you think maybe I shouldn't have asked?
        Jason Dai: No, you did well.
        Jason Dai: I'd do the same if I were in the cockpit.
    ->I thought you'd pursue it to the end.
        Jason Dai: Right.
        Jason Dai: I'd do the same if I were in the cockpit.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Jason Dai: Free agent of Grandma...
    Jason Dai: That's top secret, provided she's not lying to us.
    Jason Dai: In that case, she does have a good reason to keep us in the dark.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->But you think she's lying?
        Jason Dai: No.
        Jason Dai: I believe her.
    ->You believe her?
        Jason Dai: ...
        Jason Dai: I do.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>
//if只有問到有公司
<<#If($SiTuRealMaster=1)>>
    Jason Dai: She made it sound extraordinarily scary...
    Jason Dai: We have a good reason to believe it's all smoke and mirrors, no?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Yeah, now I trust her even less.
        Jason Dai: Agreed.
    ->I suppose there must be a grain of truth in it.
        Jason Dai: Yeah...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Jason Dai: ...
    Jason Dai: She did admit to having a corporate sponsor.
    Jason Dai: If she wanted to deceive us, she could have kept that from us, too.
<<#EndIf>>
//if被勸退啥都沒問
<<#If($SiTuRealMaster=0)>>
    Jason Dai: She made it sound extraordinarily scary...
    Jason Dai: We have a good reason to believe it's all smoke and mirrors, no?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Yeah, now I trust her even less.
        Jason Dai: Agreed.
    ->I suppose there must be a grain of truth in it.
        Jason Dai: Yeah...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Jason Dai: ...
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Do you think I should have pursued it to the end, despite her warnings?
        Jason Dai: ...
        Jason Dai: I would if I were in the cockpit.
        Jason Dai: But really, I don't know if it's the right thing to do.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>
//IF完全沒問
<<#If($SiTuRealMaster=-1)>>
    Jason Dai: Chameleon and Helper both have a good reason to deceive us.
    Jason Dai: ...
    Jason Dai: But that doesn't necessarily mean they did so.
    Jason Dai: ...
<<#EndIf>>
Jason Dai: ...
Jason Dai: Let's approach it from another angle.
Jason Dai: Suppose, in the worst case scenario, that Situ is in fact working for one of Vision's competitors.
Jason Dai: She still has no reason to hurt us.
Jason Dai: The worst she could do is to leave us behind when things start to get hectic.
Jason Dai: ...
Jason Dai: Based on what I have seen, she won't even do that.
Jason Dai: She's keeping secrets alright, but she's still essentially an altruist.
Jason Dai: Like at the construction site, she didn't bolt like Helper did.
Jason Dai: Instead, she confronted Chameleon and his men, all by herself, for a bunch of random kids.
Jason Dai: I have trouble imagining her working for a corporation for profit.
Jason Dai: What do you think?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->I don't think she's lying about her motive.
    Jason Dai: Yeah, we have an agreement on that.
    //if選不可信任
    <<#If($DecideTrustSituOrNot=-1)>>
        Jason Dai: You didn't trust her yesterday.
        Jason Dai: Today's events must have changed your mind.
    <<#EndIf>>
->I don't believe in altruists. They only exist in fairytales.
    Jason Dai: Is that so?
    //if昨天選司徒可信任
    <<#If($DecideTrustSituOrNot=1)>>
        Jason Dai: But you said you trusted Situ yesterday.
        Jason Dai: You change your mind pretty quick...
    <<#EndIf>>
    Jason Dai: ...
    Jason Dai: Nah, maybe I'm the fool for trusting her.
<<#EndSimple>>
Jason Dai: ...
Jason Dai: Well, reality is what it is...
Jason Dai: Trust Situ or not, she's our only chance of survival.
Jason Dai: We have to go with the flow, I guess.
Jason Dai: Tsk.
Jason Dai: Never having a say in my own life.
<<#End>>

title: 到樓下
body: //主角瞬移坐到隆生旁邊 Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: What kind of person do you think Nin is?;<> Thought: An independent soul.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->A doer. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Yeah, she's like that. ->A stubborn mule. Lawrence Chang: Haha, I thought you'd say that. ->An agent of justice. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Justice, huh... ->An independent soul. Lawrence Chang: Yeah, very true... Lawrence Chang: They all say that. <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: You know how I idolize her. I see her as a hero. Lawrence Chang: Now I'm starting to think maybe I'm wrong.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah, you are. Lawrence Chang: ...Why?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She is not perfect. ->Idolization is always followed by disillusion. Lawrence Chang: That... Lawrence Chang: That is right but I don't think it's the key of the issue... Thought: ... Lawrence Chang: The thing with Nin is that...;<> ->She's no hero. Lawrence Chang: ...!? Lawrence Chang: Yes! Lawrence Chang: That's it! <> <<#EndSimple>> ->How so? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: She is not what I expected... <<#Simple(8)>> ->What do you mean? Lawrence Chang: Um...;<> Lawrence Chang: I mean literally what I said. <> ->What did you expect her to be? Lawrence Chang: A true hero...?;<> Lawrence Chang: I know this sounds like child talk, haha... Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: But I seriously mean it.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: When I first got to know her, she told me that... Lawrence Chang: "This world will slowly kill itself if nobody is there to make changes." Lawrence Chang: "Once you realize this, you're one of the few that the world counts on.";<> Lawrence Chang: "If you stay out of it, you're contributing to the world's ultimate demise.";<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: That convinced me. I decided to follow her to the end of the world. Lawrence Chang: All this time, she's risking her own life to fight injustice. Lawrence Chang: Either through OWL or as herself, she never backs down. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: But this time it's different.;<> Lawrence Chang: Nin is hesitating. I'm sure of it.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You think she's protecting Vision? Lawrence Chang: Impossible. Lawrence Chang: It's more like... How should I put this... ->She's doing better than 23. Lawrence Chang: 23 is always hesitating. Lawrence Chang: We should compare Nin to her usual self. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: She's holding back from the very start, using excuses like insufficient evidence and such...;<> Lawrence Chang: We had everything necessary to arrest Shane Yan, but she chose not to. Lawrence Chang: We have found out Vision's illegal deals but barely done anything about it. Lawrence Chang: This operation has been taking one detour after another. Are we dancing around something? I don't know.;<> Lawrence Chang: It's as if Nin doesn't really care about stopping Vision. Lawrence Chang: Don't you think so, Jason?;<> Lawrence Chang: About what she did? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She gave an explanation to everything you don't agree with. ->She's maybe a little weird, but nothing out of the ordinary yet. ->I don't think there's anything wrong with her. Lawrence Chang: We had the holographic recording. Why didn't she arrest Yan? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She told you. Yan is only the tip of the iceberg. Lawrence Chang: But we still take a chunk off the iceberg, no? ->What purpose would that serve? Lawrence Chang: Purpose? How about putting one murderer behind the bars? <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: We had more than enough evidence for an arrest. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That won't stop Vision. Don't forget our real objective. ->Then what? Watch the rest of them burn evidence and flee? <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Um... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Our objective was to save flop contributors from potential harm. ->The murder attempt on Fran was not related to Vision's scheme. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: I know that, but...;<> Lawrence Chang: ... <<#Simple(8)>> ->What's the problem then, if you know that? ->Then why are you asking? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ...How would you explain today?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What's wrong with today? // ->Those kids? Lawrence Chang: Yes! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: We have evidence that Vision is involved in human trafficking!;<> Lawrence Chang: Why didn't we hand it over to the police? Why rescue those kids? Lawrence Chang: So that Vision will have time to destroy evidence and severe ties? Lawrence Chang: Didn't she say we shouldn't raise any alarm?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Because those kids were in immediate danger. Lawrence Chang: Immediate danger? ->Because we did have a chance to rescue them. Lawrence Chang: Have a chance? <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: In that case, what about those contributors in the Cellar? Lawrence Chang: They were in danger, too. We had a chance to save them. Why didn't we take any action? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->We were not certain if they were really in danger. Lawrence Chang: That is right, but... <<#Simple(8)>> ->But with these kids, we are dead certain. ->Do you take action on something that may not exist at all? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Yeah... ->Not in immediate danger. It's a process that takes years. Lawrence Chang: Not immediate? How could you say that? It's cruel. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Watching them taking these kids, isn't that cruel? Lawrence Chang: I... ->There are priorities, Lawrence. Lawrence Chang: ... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Situ must do planning on the big picture. ->She's not hesitating, only having different priorities. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: OK, let's say you make a good point.;<> Lawrence Chang: Now... ->I think she's a bit too hesitant, too.;<> Lawrence Chang: Right? <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Negotiation with Vision? That I absolutely don't agree with. Never. Lawrence Chang: Letting them go can't possibly be right.;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: ...Do you agree with Nin on that, too?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sorry, but I actually do.;<> ->We have to.;<> <<@隆生抱怨主角幹嘛支持司徒寧>> Lawrence Chang: Not you, too? //if剛剛覺得保守 <<#If($AgreedSiTuConservative=0)>> Lawrence Chang: Didn't you just say Nin's too hesitant? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I did, but this is a whole different matter. ->She's hesitant, but not necessarily wrong. <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: You lost me. <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->We have to do it, for the sake of the victims. ->It's a crappy solution, but also the least crappy option in our toolbox. ->She did it for the safety of Fran and I.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: What... Lawrence Chang: What about the murder attempt on Ms. Cheng? Do you just let it slide?;<> Lawrence Chang: Let Yan and Chameleon keep doing what they do? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I care more about Fran's present and future. ->Anything that gets our old life back. title: 第四日程芳談話
body: //暗e以後主角瞬到程芳旁邊微蹲伏它，程芳薇薇撐起自己。接著程芳淡入到床上，主角同時淡入到坐在床上。 Frances Cheng: Phew...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->How are you feeling? Frances Cheng: I'm good.;<> ->It's been a long day for you. Frances Cheng: Not really. Your day is longer. Haha.;<> ->...;<> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: The situation is quickly spinning out of control... <<#Simple(8)>> ->But for good or bad, tomorrow will be the end of it. Frances Cheng: I have the same feeling. ->Are you scared? Frances Cheng: Scared...?;<> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I'm kind of getting used to it.;<> Thought: Fran... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Hey, J. Frances Cheng: ...Do you think I'm too bossy today? <> <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Bossy? Frances Cheng: I mean... Frances Cheng: I made a lot of demands to Ms. Situ?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I don't think so. ->You were alright. Frances Cheng: Yeah. ->A little bit, maybe?;<> Frances Cheng: *Sigh* <<#EndSimple>> ->Not at all. Frances Cheng: Really?;<> ->A little bit, maybe? Frances Cheng: *Sigh*;<> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Was I too mean? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not at all. ->No! What are you talking about? Frances Cheng: Honestly? Frances Cheng: Phew...;<> ->Probably a little bit mean.;<> Frances Cheng: You think so? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->But within the reasonable range. Frances Cheng: Huh...;<> ->Yeah, you were pretty mean back then. Frances Cheng: *Sigh*;<> ->I was kidding! Frances Cheng: Hey! I'm asking a serious question! <> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I've been doubting, pushing them. Frances Cheng: First for failing to protect you. Frances Cheng: Then for hiding things from us. Frances Cheng: But unlike you, I was not working in the field, so I guess I don't have a right to say that.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Your concerns are legitimate. ->I get the feeling, but no such thing. You're just worrying too much. Frances Cheng: Haha, thank you. Frances Cheng: But again, you're my boyfriend, of course you'd say what I want to hear.;<> Thought: Would I...? <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: You should keep your own perspective.;<> Frances Cheng: Don't just agree with me on everything. My opinion is only for your reference. Frances Cheng: Geez, I'm nagging...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thank you for caring so much about me, Fran. I'm happy to hear you nagging. Frances Cheng: ...For real? //if剛剛說有一點 <<#If($DLStateFloat=1)>> Frances Cheng: But you just said I'm a bit bossy... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->A tiny little bit doesn't count. Frances Cheng: No, it counts. Haha! ->I was joking. Frances Cheng: Aha. It's not funny. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Okay, I guess I'll just believe you for now!;<> <<#EndIf>> //if剛剛沒說有一點 <<#If($DLStateFloat=-1)>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->For real. Frances Cheng: Great! ->Nope. I'm kidding. Frances Cheng: Hey! Haha! <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> ->Don't worry. I can take care of myself. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Good!;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Hey, have you realized? We switched roles. Frances Cheng: You used to do all the nagging, telling me not to do this or that.;<> Frances Cheng: Now it's exactly the opposite.;<> Frances Cheng: I know how you used to feel, wanting and not wanting to tell me what to do at the same time. Frances Cheng: But I think I'm more reasonable than you were?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I guess. Frances Cheng: Great!;<> ->No. I'm more cooperative than you were. Frances Cheng: No way! Haha!;<> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I'm worried.;<> Frances Cheng: About the mission tomorrow. Frances Cheng: I know you're much tougher than the average guy... Frances Cheng: But against what we will face tomorrow? I'm worried about you...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I will be OK, Fran. Frances Cheng: I believe you! ->I'll bolt once things start to go south. Frances Cheng: Ha! You'd better. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: Hearing about your condition from Ms. Situ, it feels so unreal... Frances Cheng: All this talk about possession, persona, and mental space... Frances Cheng: What do you think?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It feels unreal, yeah. Frances Cheng: But you're taking it surprisingly well.;<> <<#GoTo(@程芳說自己反應很大)>> ->I'm taking it rather well. Frances Cheng: You are. You deal with the world rather well in general.;<> <<@程芳說自己反應很大>> Frances Cheng: I've seldom seen you surprised. Frances Cheng: I, on the other hand, am making a big deal out of everything...;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->This is a big deal. ->I'm just born with a poker face. Frances Cheng: Haha! You are, yes.;<> Frances Cheng: A poker face has its perks. ->I'd like to be you for a change. Frances Cheng: Oh?;<> Frances Cheng: Do you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Totally. Frances Cheng: Ha, I see!;<> ->Nope. I'm kidding. Frances Cheng: Hey! You can't just take it back like that!;<> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->What if I told you that I'm not Jason Dai?;//GLITCH+1 <> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: This is not funny, OK? Frances Cheng: Situ said she installed something in you, to block out possession.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Somehow that thing is not working.;//GLITCH+1 <> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: You mean, for real? Frances Cheng: You...;<> Frances Cheng: You really are not Jason? Frances Cheng: You... //glitch最強主角本人搶走控制權 <> Jason Dai: Sorry. Jason Dai: That was a bad joke, yeah. Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: Don't do that again!;<> ->She did. I'm joking. Frances Cheng: That's a crappy joke!;<> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Not just crappy, it's creepy! Frances Cheng: Heh...;<> Frances Cheng: My nerves can't take this now! <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sorry, I won't do it again. ->Yes, ma'am. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Hmph!;<> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Thought: Fran's turning gloomy again... Frances Cheng: When we're done with Vision, you will have to deal with OWL. Frances Cheng: If you can't complete the training... <<#Simple(8)>> ->I'll nail the training if there's no other way. Frances Cheng: I believe you can.;<> ->How about we just run away? Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: We can't.;<> Frances Cheng: I haven't seen many OWL agents yet. Frances Cheng: But if they're all like Ms. Situ or 23... Frances Cheng: Do we even have a chance?;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Once you join OWL, we won't be free anymore. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What do you mean? // ->I thought the whole point of joining OWL is to find freedom? Frances Cheng: No. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: As an agent, you will have access to many secrets.;<> Frances Cheng: Scandals of both corporations and governments.;<> Frances Cheng: OWL can't just leave you be. There must be some kind of surveillance or monitoring system. Frances Cheng: Everyone close to you will be closely watched, too. Frances Cheng: If necessary, they may even use me as a leverage against you.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->But Situ didn't mention any of this. Frances Cheng: I guess she didn't want to.;<> Frances Cheng: It doesn't matter, though, because we don't have any alternative either way. Frances Cheng: Knowing everything at this stage could be counterproductive. Frances Cheng: That fits her way of thinking.;<> ->Maybe it's not that bad. Frances Cheng: Maybe.;<> Frances Cheng: Although I think it may be even worse than that. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: But, Fran...;<> Frances Cheng: I know what you're thinking, J.;<> Frances Cheng: "If I'm to join OWL, Fran won't be able to go to the march." Right?;<> Frances Cheng: Yes, I will lose many things that I used to be passionate about.;<> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: But it's not your fault! Don't blame yourself.;<> Frances Cheng: If I really value them that much, I could choose to break up with you any time. Frances Cheng: But truth is, I value you over anything else. Frances Cheng: I will be with you, no matter what. Frances Cheng: This is my choice. Frances Cheng: Feeling guilty means you're doubting my own choice. Frances Cheng: You hear that? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Gotcha. Frances Cheng: Yeah, good! ->But I really don't want to put restrictions on you. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I know. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: J...;<> Frances Cheng: I want you to respect my choices. Frances Cheng: In return, I will respect yours. Frances Cheng: Going forward, always remember this.;<> Frances Cheng: Whatever you choose, I will be by your side. Frances Cheng: We will face it together. Frances Cheng: Shall we?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes. Frances Cheng: Great! ->I love you. Frances Cheng: ...I love you, too. <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>>
