title: 回到司徒基地3
body: <> Nin Situ: Chameleon got away. 23: Nin... Nin Situ: They betrayed us. They're letting Chameleon go.;<> Thought: She's burning with rage, on the verge of eruption. Nin Situ: OWL called off all reinforcements and told me to can it. 23: You went after his alone? Nin Situ: A wild-goose chase. 23: What the hell do you think you're doing!;<>;<>;<>;<>;<>;//23驚訝站起 23: You were already injured! Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: They don't have a reason to protect Chameleon. It must be the information that he knows.;<>;<> Nin Situ: If OWL is so determined to keep Vision's secrets, we must take a more invasive approach.;<> 23: ...Invasive?;<>;<> 23: We should just give up!;<> 23: Since OWL is going this far to cover up for Vision...;<> 23: You know what that indicates, Nin.;<>;<> Nin Situ: ...;<>;<> Nin Situ: Human experiment on minors. Hiring wanted fugitive. Nin Situ: If Vision is willing to go this far, the return must be much more than the risk. Think about this, 23!;<>;<> 23: I know, I know.;<> Nin Situ: Worse still, they keep pushing the boundary.;<> Nin Situ: If we let them go, they will start to think this is safe and do even worse things next time! 23: I know that, too. It's just—;<> Nin Situ: We are the only thing standing against them. If anyone is able to do something, it's us. 23: I know. I just—;<>;<> Nin Situ: Now that we're all clear, let's discuss tomorrow's mission.;<>;<> 23: I just can't see you hurt. <> <<#Wait(2)>>;//23這裏哭哭 23: ...;<>;<> Thought: 23? What's happening?;<> Thought: Is she sobbing? <>;<> //if有告訴他可撤退 <<#If($ToldCanRetreat=0)>> 23: ... 23: You had a chance to pull out! But you— Thought: Situ gave me a cold stare.;<> 23: Why do you keep doing this!;<>;<> 23: What's wrong with you? I don't understand! Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I'm sorry, but I did what I had to do.;<>;<> Nin Situ: The chance of them killing me was very slim, and I am here to prove it. <<#EndIf>> Nin Situ: I thought we were done with this.;<> Nin Situ: Getting hurt is part of the job.;<> Nin Situ: We will die, we both, most likely not in a bed.;<> 23: I don't mean that.;<>;<>;<> 23: I mean mentally.;<> Nin Situ: ...Mentally?;<>;<> Nin Situ: Have you ever seen me having mental issue? 23: No, but it only makes me more worried.;<> 23: You're like a pressure cooker. When you eventually blow up, the aftermath will be horrible.;<> Nin Situ: I...;<>;<>;<> Nin Situ: I don't have time for this. No.;<> Nin Situ: Leave that for another time. Now, tomorrow's mission.;<> Nin Situ: Shall we?;<> Nin Situ: ...;<>;<> 23: Yes.;<> 23: ... Nin Situ: Hey, look at me, 23.;<> Nin Situ: If you need a break, take one.;<>;<> Nin Situ: I can't see you being tormented by these thoughts.;<> Nin Situ: Take a break. Give yourself a couple of weeks. That is, unless you're sure you can keep going. Nin Situ: Think about it. Nin Situ: Can you still do this?;<> 23: Phew...;<>;<> 23: Yeah, I'm good.;//這具說謊台左手 23: I'm OK now.;<>;//維持台左手 23: Sorry, I lost control for a second.;<> <> //if沒有告訴他可撤退 <<#If($ToldCanRetreat=-1)>> 23: I thought you were going to die— Nin Situ: Don't apologize for your feelings. 23: Yeah, I know. <<#EndIf>> 23: ... 23: Yep, I'm all good now.;<>;<> 23: You'll take care of everything at the end of the day!;<>;<>;//這具說謊台左手 Nin Situ: ...;<>;<> 23: Ahem! 23: Alright, I am my old self again.;<>;<> 23: Chill and relax!;<>;//維持台左手 Nin Situ: Good.;<>;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: Now, I'll begin with the briefing.;<> Nin Situ: Today we failed to apprehend Chameleon. However, I managed to attach a tracker to him while we brawled. Nin Situ: Before going dark, the tracker showed he went to a facility a lot like Silencio's Cellar. A huge underground facility. Nin Situ: It's also a lot larger than the Cellar that you know. I've sent the data over, 23.;<> 23: ...;<>;<>;<> 23: What's this...? 23: It's beneath Vision's industrial zone alright, but there's no record of this structure anywhere.;<> 23: Judging by the scale of structure and its power consumption, it should be a research facility of sorts. Frances Cheng: Maybe one of the Red Room's secret labs?;<>;<>;<> Frances Cheng: I know they have unregistered labs, but something of this size?;<> 23: Oh?;<> 23: Lemme see...;<>;<> 23: Maybe you are onto something, F.;<> 23: Lots of access records by Red Room personnel. 23: ...!;<> 23: This place has network cables connecting to basically all of Vision's flop farms! Frances Cheng: ...That means!;<>;<> Nin Situ: It could be the source of our unexplained flops.;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Ha! We solved the puzzle!;<> Nin Situ: Not so fast. We don't know what exactly is going on inside. Nin Situ: We must infiltrate the facility, now. 23: ...Now? Frances Cheng: Why such hurry? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: My tracker also has a bug in it. It captured some conversations.;<> Lawrence Chang: What did he say? Nin Situ: He was giving orders to his men, and in the process mentioned something.;<> Nin Situ: Piecing it together, I have reasons to believe there are about 300 human test subjects, held against their wills.;<> Nin Situ: They are running an experiment of sorts, on increasing flop output by stimulation of pain. 23: Meaning...;<>;<> 23: They're running a medieval dungeon full of torture chambers?;<>;<> Frances Cheng: Dear gods...;<> Lawrence Chang: These fuckers are sick! 23: This is what they're trying so hard to hide...;<> 23: Did they generate so many flops with that?;<>;<> 23: Is this Project Sleeping God?;<> //if有猜到是葉慈恩 <<#If($GuessYehIsVillain=0)>> 23: The pieces are finally fitting together... Nin Situ: What?;<> 23: I haven't got a chance to share this yet. 23: The short story is, thanks to Jason...;<>;<> 23: I've found someone in Silencio who has been in touch with the Red Room for a long time.;<> 23: Your guess was spot-on, Jason. It's Edith Yeh. 23: The pro-closure faction used her as their snitch. In the process she learned about Project Sleeping God.;<> Nin Situ: What did you find on her?;<> 23: Project Sleeping God started decades ago, before Silencio was even built.;<> 23: Vision built a gigantic facility for this project alone. 23: That should be that dungeon we just found. Nin Situ: So they need a continuous supply of test subjects...;<> <<#EndIf>> Frances Cheng: Wait, something's not right. Did you say about 300 test subjects?;<>;<> Frances Cheng: Where did they come from?;<> Nin Situ: Most likely from the Shithole, through human trafficking or abduction.;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Take hundreds from the street without anyone noticing?;<> Nin Situ: At least 80% of Shithole dwellers have no registration or family. No one cares if they're gone. Nin Situ: As long as you do it in small batches. No one's gonna find out. Frances Cheng: If they can do that...;<>;<>;<> Frances Cheng: Why are they bothering with negotiations and leases this time? 23: ...Because now they want children.;<>;<>;<> 23: If there's a child, you bet there's at least one parent. They could inform the police. Nin Situ: Or at least make noises and attract attention.;<>;<> 23: ...;<> 23: Is your bug still working, Nin? Nin Situ: It went dark just when they started to talk about the test subjects.;<> 23: Poor signal, or removed?;<> Nin Situ: I can't tell. 23: ...;<> Nin Situ: I know what you're thinking.;<>;<> Nin Situ: You're about to say it's another trap, that Chameleon wanted me to hear it and take the bait. 23: It's 60% likely, at least.;<> Nin Situ: I'd say 80%.;<>;<> 23: But you're still going, aren't you?;<> Nin Situ: Because I also believe that there's at least a 40% chance that Vision is doing inhumane experiments. 23: ...;<> 23: 80% risk for 40% payoff.;<>;<> Nin Situ: I'm willing to do it for only 10% payoff. We are talking about human lives.;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: I'm with Nin!;<>;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: What do we do? Lawrence Chang: Kick the door down and rescue everyone inside? 23: No freaking way.;<>;<>;<>;<> 23: The four of us rescuing hundreds from a gigantic underground maze that we don't have a map of? 23: And this maze happens to be heavily guarded and probably booby trapped, too?;<> 23: Think about it, Nin. Nin Situ: ...;<>;<> Nin Situ: Rescue is not part of our objective, not for tomorrow.;<> Nin Situ: My plan is a quick probe mission for evidence. Infiltration, in and out. Nin Situ: Once we get it, I'll...;<> Lawrence Chang: Publish everything! Vision has no choice but to release them by then! Nin Situ: ...;<>;//司徒寧沒有看隆生 23: Let the adults do the talk, Lawrence. Lawrence Chang: Am I wrong?;<>;<> Nin Situ: Once we publish it, the police will initiate due process. Police investigation, litigation and hearing...;<> Nin Situ: Vision will fight back every single step. Frances Cheng: The process could take years. During this time, Vision will get rid of the evidence...;<>;<>;<> 23: A large part of this evidence is the test subjects themselves, meaning... Nin Situ: They will be killed.;<>;<>;<>;<> Nin Situ: As I said, most, if not all, of these people officially don't exist. Nin Situ: Kill them and burn the bodies. No trace left. Frances Cheng: What should we do, then...? Nin Situ: ... 23: Nin...;<>;<>;<> 23: Here is the only way that I can think of. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: With the evidence we will recover tomorrow, I will start a negotiation with Vision.;<>;<> Nin Situ: We promise to keep what we find in the dark. For exchange, they must call off their experiments. Frances Cheng: Why would they agree? What if any of these test subjects decide to talk to the police or the press? Nin Situ: Vision will agree, because the commercial and political risks are too high. Nin Situ: As to the problem of test subjects talking...;<> Nin Situ: Talk all they want. No one will take the hallucination of a tripping Shithole dweller seriously.;<> Nin Situ: Jason, Frances. This negotiation is also for the safety of you both. Nin Situ: As part of the deal, I'll tell Vision to leave you alone. <<#Simple(8)>> ->And I just trust Vision on their word? ->I don't believe them. Nin Situ: Rest assured. Vision does mind you as much as you may think.;<> Nin Situ: Particularly, against the risk of me publishing our findings, you are no threat at all. Nin Situ: Vision don't do what they do just for fun. Nin Situ: Fundamentally it boils down to costs and gains. It's not worth it. Nin Situ: Once I put the offer on the table, the cost of harming either of you will be ridiculously high.;<> Nin Situ: They have no reason to keep bugging you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: I think it's a good plan.;<>;<> Thought: Fran... Thought: As long as the safety of Fran is guaranteed... Lawrence Chang: Bullshit!;<>;<>;<>;<>;<>;//隆生激動站立 Nin Situ: Lawrence... Lawrence Chang: This is bullshit!;<> Lawrence Chang: After everything that Vision did, you're now telling me they can walk away for free? Lawrence Chang: No punishment at all? As if nothing happened?;<> Lawrence Chang: What the hell are we doing this for? Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: I...;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Lawrence, this is for the maximum interest of everyone combined. Lawrence Chang: Yes, including Vision's interest.;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Are we done now? Nin Situ: Yes. Meeting adjourned. We will cover the specifics tomorrow morning. Lawrence Chang: I'm going.;<>;<> //隆生離開房間。 <<#End>> <<#End>>

title: 司徒單獨談2
body: Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: A question, Ms. Situ.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Yes, please.;<> Frances Cheng: In the middle of a possession, if the original awareness is confined to this space...;<> Frances Cheng: Does that mean there's another awareness taking over the rest of the mind?;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: Yes.;<>;<> Nin Situ: We are not remote controlled like toys—press this button to do this thing, not like that. Nin Situ: It's more like spiritual possession, being invaded and controlled by an external awareness. Frances Cheng: The original awareness is shut off in that space, where it could meet...;<> Nin Situ: The invader? Frances Cheng: Yeah... Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: That is known to happen.;<> Nin Situ: In my case, mostly when my body is asleep. Nin Situ: My original awareness, relinquishing control due to obvious reasons, might let the invader in. Nin Situ: But with training, I was able to pull the invader into that space, even when my body is awake. Frances Cheng: Is that possible?;<> Nin Situ: ...Yes.;<> Nin Situ: I could do it, at least...;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: That was the last time that I was possessed. Nin Situ: I was physically at the OWL HQ, behind Grandma, facing against me.;<> Nin Situ: Then I felt this force, sucking my soul out of my body. Suddenly all physical sensations left me. Nin Situ: I saw my body reaching for my sidearm, raising the weapon, taking aim... Nin Situ: It was like in a movie theatre. I could only sit and watch. Nin Situ: Suddenly I felt this rage. The next thing I knew, I pulled the invader into my little space. Nin Situ: That thing was equally surprised. It didn't say anything. Nin Situ: ... Frances Cheng: What happened then? Nin Situ: I strangled it.;<> //主角突然頭痛+glitch Thought: Huh!?;<> Nin Situ: Then I unlocked the full potential of this body, and was never possessed again. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Strangled? You can strangle a thing without a physical body? Nin Situ: That I'm not sure. Nin Situ: In my mind it felt like strangling something alive. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: I...;//glitch+1 Thought: I could...;<> Thought: ... Frances Cheng: Jason? Thought: ...;//glitch+1 Frances Cheng: ...Are you alright, J?;<> Frances Cheng: Jason!?;//大叫 <> //glitch停下 <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm OK. ->Lost myself for a second. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Ms. Situ, is that normal?;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I think he will do it faster than I expected.;<> Frances Cheng: I assume it's a good thing? Nin Situ: Yes.;<> Frances Cheng: Phew...;<>;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Jason.;<> Nin Situ: I'm doing this for you, partly, mostly out of goodwill.;<> Nin Situ: I want to see you safe, my kin. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah, thank you. // ->We are not kin. Nin Situ: Apologies if you don't like that word. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I don't like it. // ->I didn't mean that. Nin Situ: Don't worry about it. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I see you as my kin, nonetheless. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Another reason is that I believe this is the right thing to do. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Why bring this up? // ->The right thing? Nin Situ: You didn't choose to be engineered. Hence you shouldn't be punished for it. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: What happens next could be very complicated. Nin Situ: Once OWL steps in, you won't have many choices. Nin Situ: I hope you, Jason and Frances, could trust me. <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I trust you. ->I trust you, for now. <> Nin Situ: Thank you.;<> Nin Situ: That is good enough. ->Sorry, but I can't. ->I don't know if I should. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I understand.;<> Nin Situ: I won't stop you if you end up choosing none of the paths I provide. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: Sorry, Ms. Situ, I can't fully trust you.;<> Thought: Fran... Nin Situ: I... Frances Cheng: I don't understand your values.;<> Frances Cheng: Worse still, I don't think you understand yourself, either. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: It should be very clear by now. My value is to do the right thing. Frances Cheng: As if this vague slogan means anything. Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: I'm willing to go to any length to do what I believe is right. Nin Situ: That includes what's normally seen as criminal, inhumane, or unreasonable. Nin Situ: There is such thing as the necessary evil. The world is not a perfect place. We make trade-offs. Nin Situ: The thing with a trade-off is that...;<> Nin Situ: Everything must carry a price tag. Or else you don't know if you're better or worse off.;<> Nin Situ: The process of coming up with these price tags might appear to be... Frances Cheng: Heartless. Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: ...Indeed.;<> Frances Cheng: That much I understand. Frances Cheng: What are your standards? How do you determine the value of something?;<>;<> Frances Cheng: I can't trust you without understanding how you see the world. Nin Situ: It's hard to tell in a few sentences.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Because my standards change. Nin Situ: I see what you call "justice" as a fluctuating line. Nin Situ: The only thing constant about it is that it's inconstant. <<#Simple(8)>> ->How are we supposed to trust you have your values are so fluid? Nin Situ: That question is better left for yourselves. ->How are people supposed to trust you, then? Nin Situ: Each person has a different answer. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: There's something that I can tell you, though.;<> Nin Situ: What we call "trust" is in fact a form of predictability. Nin Situ: You "trust" me because you think my behaviors are predictable. You know I'll keep my promises. Nin Situ: No one trusts an unpredictable person.;<> Nin Situ: However, like said before, I don't believe in rigid things like principles.;<> Nin Situ: I make every judgment as a separate case. Nin Situ: My point is...;<> Nin Situ: There's one thing about me that you can count on. Whatever I do, I absolutely believe it's the right thing. Nin Situ: That never changes. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Then what's your definition of the "right thing"? Nin Situ: We're running in circles. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: There's only one way for you to trust me. Nin Situ: By keeping up with my values. Nin Situ: I pursue justice, or the "right thing". Justice is fluid, so I am fluid. Nin Situ: Partly because I live and learn, constantly correcting my misconceptions. Also, the standard of justice changes over time, too. Nin Situ: The bottom line is, I don't believe justice stays in one place. Thought: This is exactly what Helper said... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Meaning you may abandon us someday. Nin Situ: Yes.;<> Nin Situ: But if you keep up with my values... Nin Situ: You will see that day coming, and will understand why I do it. ->Isn't that a cheap excuse for double standard? Nin Situ: It's one consistently optimizing standard.;<> Nin Situ: Reflect, assess, fix mistakes, and repeat. Nin Situ: Holding onto the same standard traps you in time.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I see.;<>;<> Frances Cheng: Now I choose to trust you, Nin. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Thank you, Frances.;<>;<> //if剛剛說不信任 <<#If($DLStateFloat=-1)>> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I choose to trust you, too. Nin Situ: Thank you, Jason.;<> ->I won't say I trust you just yet. Nin Situ: It's OK. You're entitled to your judgments.;<> Nin Situ: Being cautious is not a bad thing. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: We should all take some rest. Frances Cheng: One more thing, Nin. Nin Situ: What is it?;<> Frances Cheng: ...You concern is reasonable, that Lawrence may take today's events the wrong way. Frances Cheng: I kept asking if you're rational, because to me, your decisions seem very emotional.;<> Frances Cheng: I feel that way, even when I know you're an almost perfectly rational person. Frances Cheng: What you said was not convincing enough. Frances Cheng: We all judge situations from our own experiences and perspectives.;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Frances Cheng: You know you're calm, doesn't mean everyone else will believe you. Frances Cheng: They don't believe it's possible, at least not possible for them. Nin Situ: ...Are you trying to warn me? Frances Cheng: ...;<>;<> Frances Cheng: I'm not sure, but... Frances Cheng: You are a perfect person, but your partners are not. Frances Cheng: Please don't forget the difference.;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: I'm not perfect. Far from it.;<> Nin Situ: But I know what you mean.;<> Nin Situ: I will pay more notice. Thank you.;<> Frances Cheng: Yeah!;<> Nin Situ: *Cough*;<> //司徒寧劇烈咳嗽，看了一下自己的手(手上有血) Thought: She's coughing up blood!;<> Frances Cheng: Nin! Nin Situ: ...I'm alright. //司徒寧看著自己的手 Nin Situ: I just...;<> Nin Situ: ...Just need some rest. Frances Cheng: Are you really alright? Nin Situ: I am. Engineered humans like us... Nin Situ: *Cough*;<> Nin Situ: We heal up pretty fast. Nin Situ: Some patching up and rest should do the job. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Need a hand? Nin Situ: No. ->I'll let 23 know. Nin Situ: Please don't. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Trust me, I'm alright.;<> Nin Situ: Let's call it a day. You two need rest, too. Nin Situ: Good night. Frances Cheng: Good night.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Good night. // ->You really don't need a hand? Nin Situ: I don't. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>>

title: 麵攤2
body: Ivan Zhang: ...You said a table for two.;<>;<> 23: Took you a while.;<> Ivan Zhang: How many tag-alongs do you take with you?;<> //if有跟陳木海找張延安聊過 <<#If($HadWoodSeaChangTalk=0)>> Ivan Zhang: Hold on. You're...!? Thought: Ivan Zhang's face says he just saw the most ridiculous thing in the whole wide world... 23: What? You know Jason? Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Yeah, he's one of our patrons... 23: Ah. <<#EndIf>> 23: Where's the thing? Ivan Zhang: Same old place. 23: Genuine Sunrise stuff? Ivan Zhang: Don't trust Ivan the Fix-It Man?;<> 23: Wouldn't even dream of it.;<>;<>;<> 23: Just wanna see if you're keeping to the usual standard. Ivan Zhang: ...;<>;<> Ivan Zhang: Same old deal, same old thing. Don't you worry about it.;<>;<> Ivan Zhang: Tell that Situ to use it with care. These things are not supposed to be disposable. Ivan Zhang: At this rate I can't keep you supplied. Don't mind the stream of money, though.;<> 23: Ha, do you think she'd listen?;<> Ivan Zhang: Yeah, you'll have an easier time convincing a missile.;<> Ivan Zhang: Enough business talk! Now eat your noodles.;<> 23: A couple more questions, then I'm done.;<>;<>;<> Ivan Zhang: ... 23: Where have you been? Couldn't find you for a while. Ivan Zhang: ...;<> Ivan Zhang: Business trip. Need to restock. So what? 23: Just a friendly reminder. Be careful dealing with the big potatoes. They bite.;<> Ivan Zhang: Sunrise? I wouldn't call them big potatoes.;<>;<> 23: You know I mean someone else.;<> Ivan Zhang: ...;<>;<> 23: Keep your business small and steady. Don't touch anything too far above your class.;<> 23: You know the risk. Ivan Zhang: ...;<> 23: This is for your own good, old man. 23: Don't say I didn't warn you.;<> Ivan Zhang: You're the customer and the customer is always right.;<>;<> 23: No, I mean it, seriously. Ivan Zhang: Tell yourself that first. Ivan Zhang: I know you're screwing with something big, or else you wouldn't need me. 23: We are being very cautious.;<> Ivan Zhang: Hmph. Eat your noodles.;<> //老張淡出去抽菸 <> 23: ...;<>;<> 23: The same grumpy old man. He'll be alright.;<> Lawrence Chang: Who is he? 23: Weapon smuggler. Works with us all the time. I need to order something for tomorrow.;<> 23: He knows people in Sunrise. Nin likes Sunrise stuff. Sturdy, and packs a serious punch. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why are you buying from smugglers? 23: OWL doesn't know about this mission, is why.;<> 23: We're paying out of our own pocket. 23: My hard-earned money! <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: You're no stranger to these deals, Lawrence. Lawrence Chang: Uh-huh. Saw you do it a couple of times.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: I've never personally been in one of these deals though. 23: They only deal with patrons, which you are not.;<> Lawrence Chang: Surely every patron starts somewhere? 23: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Nin just doesn't trust me. She wouldn't listen back there, either.;<> 23: Hey... 23: How much longer do you need to brood over this, boy?;<> 23: I'm sick of it.;<> 23: Once again, she does trust you!;<> Lawrence Chang: You didn't see the way she jabbed me with her stare!;<> Lawrence Chang: You brought up the bug in Ms. Cheng's wound, then she's immediately looking my way. Thought: Did she? <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Stare how? Lawrence Chang: It's like... Lawrence Chang: ...Like she's disappointed. 23: ... ->Aren't you imagining it? I didn't notice anything like that. Lawrence Chang: Because she wasn't looking at you! ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: You're paranoid.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: It should have been me inside the construction site. But she chose Jason. Lawrence Chang: I could have held the fort, but she insisted on waiting for you. Lawrence Chang: I've been trying too hard to help in this way or that. She keeps saying no. You do, too.;<> Lawrence Chang: All this talk about trusting me, why don't you show it? Lawrence Chang: Why do you keep me outside this operation? Lawrence Chang: Does she think I'm a snitch or what? 23: Hey, your imagination's running too wild. Lawrence Chang: And now, she's gonna let Vision go!?;<> Lawrence Chang: I don't understand! What's wrong with her!? Lawrence Chang: Do we just let them go like that?;<> Lawrence Chang: When we know all about their dirty deals!?;<> Lawrence Chang: Say something, 23! 23: ...;<> 23: Show some patience. 23: You don't understand her judgment because you're not ready.;<> Lawrence Chang: Not ready? For what?;<> Lawrence Chang: Not ready to join OWL? What does that have to do with what I just said!?;<> 23: I didn't mean... Lawrence Chang: Didn't you say I'm not ready? Lawrence Chang: So you really mean I'm not capable! 23: It's not about capability...;<> Lawrence Chang: Then what is it about?;<>;//拍桌站起 23: ... Lawrence Chang: WHAT! 23: ... Lawrence Chang: Don't you have nothing to say!? Lawrence Chang: Huh? 23!?;<> 23: Aaaaarrgggh! Shut the hell up!;<> 23: It's your mentality!;<> 23: Hear me now? Your mentality! Lawrence Chang: I don't get it.;<>;<> 23: You're an emotional, impulsive, immature brat. 23: You don't think things through. Instead, you act on what feels like a good idea at the time. 23: Most of the time you focus on what you want things to be, not what they may end up being. 23: No direction, no values, just impulses, rushing this way and that.;<> 23: We are dealing with a delicate situation. One step wrong, and the result could be nuclear fallout.;<> 23: This hate of yours against the flop farming industry will ruin it all. Lawrence Chang: I... 23: We have been stressing over and over again, that no personal emotions in operations.;<>;<> 23: There are red lines to avoid, trade-offs to make. 23: Good intention doesn't justify collateral damage! 23: You refuse to listen, OK. Suit yourself. We don't have time to wait for you to grow up.;<> 23: More importantly, we can't afford to let you screw up this operation. Lawrence Chang: Screw up? I won't—;<> 23: You won't?;<> 23: Do you think I'm blind or what? 23: Didn't I know about your little shtick at the Flop Core?;<> <> //if有告訴司徒隆生偷跑且有告訴隆昇自己告密 <<#If($ToldRonMCNark=0)>> Lawrence Chang: ... 23: Right. Nin told me all about it. <<#GoTo(@跳過隆生告密情況檢定環節)>> <<#EndIf>> //if有告訴司徒隆生偷跑 <<#If($LieAboutRonDindntGoByHimself=false)>> Lawrence Chang: How could you—!?;<> 23: Not his fault! Asking him to keep it secret only makes your issue worse. Lawrence Chang: ... <> <<#EndIf>> //if沒告訴司徒隆生偷跑 <<#If($LieAboutRonDindntGoByHimself=true)>> Lawrence Chang: How could you—!?;<> 23: Don't look at him! He kept your secret well enough. 23: There's a log file in the device showing how long you stayed inside the Flop Core. Way too long.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: I didn't— 23: Really? Still trying to deny?;<> Lawrence Chang: ... <<#EndIf>> <<@跳過隆生告密情況檢定環節>> 23: Nin needs a team that follows instructions without asking.;<> 23: She needs soldiers, not a bunch of amateurs.;<> 23: Not a cry-baby who keeps talking about justice but really wants revenge.;<> 23: So, you're right!;<> 23: Nin doesn't trust you! I don't trust you! 23: Shut the hell up and learn to listen, will ya? Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Thought: Lawrence... 23: You happy now? 23: ... <<#Simple(8)>> ->That was unnecessary, 23. 23: ...He asked for it. ->Calm down, Lawrence. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ... 23: ...;<>;<> <<#End>> //等一下

title: 麵攤內心世界
body: //進入主角內心世界 //if告訴程芳自己控制主角 Jason Dai: ...The fuck!? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That's my line. ->What the hell? <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I... Jason Dai: I think I just dragged you inside... title: 麵店被叫醒(老張)2
body: Ivan Zhang: Oi. Ivan Zhang: You.;<> Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Listen.;<> Ivan Zhang: You work for Situ and 23? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->How do you know? Ivan Zhang: They buy stuff from me. Of course I know. ->Sort of. Ivan Zhang: Hmph. ->What do you want? Ivan Zhang: Relax. I don't mean you no harm. <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: I've seen my share of young men like you.;<> Ivan Zhang: Doing OWL's dirty work because they don't have a choice. They need you for snitch or muscles, yeah? <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sort of. // ->I'm not forced. Ivan Zhang: Of course they won't force you. Situ and 23 and generally good people. Ivan Zhang: Still, you are dragged into this mess they made, against your will. Am I wrong? <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: And let me guess. It involves some huge ass corporation?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I can't tell you. Ivan Zhang: Yeah. You don't have a reason to trust me and I'm alright with that. Ivan Zhang: However. ->How do you know? Ivan Zhang: I know them long enough to know their line of work. ->Wrong. Ivan Zhang: Bullshit. <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Have you thought about what happens afterwards? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Afterwards? Nothing. Ivan Zhang: No. <<#GoTo(@老張說明任務後會怎樣)>> ->Do you mean there's something bad waiting for me? Ivan Zhang: Pretty much. <<#GoTo(@老張說明任務後會怎樣)>> ->Never thought about it. <<#GoTo(@老張說明任務後會怎樣)>> ->I work for them in exchange for protection. Ivan Zhang: Before your operation ends, yes. Ivan Zhang: What about afterwards? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'll be a free man afterwards. Ivan Zhang: I like your optimism, but things don't often end that well. ->What could happen? <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<@老張說明任務後會怎樣>> Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: They will move on to other cases. You're forgotten. Ivan Zhang: Whatever aftermath this operation has, you will be the one footing the bill.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->What if there's no aftermath? Ivan Zhang: Ha! I've lived long enough and never seen one job wrapping up aftermath-free. Ivan Zhang: Think about it yourself. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: What if OWL doesn't want to leave you alone? Or whatever force you're fucking with fucks back?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'll turn to Situ and 23 for help. Ivan Zhang: Can you even get hold of them by then? Ivan Zhang: Let's say you could. How high do you think you are, on their ladder of priorities?;<> Ivan Zhang: Do you really believe they will put down whatever they're doing and come to your rescue? Thought: Situ will not care that much about me... ->What are you getting at? Say it already. <> <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: I'm saying you need a proper exit plan. Ivan Zhang: Corporations rule this world. Once they see you, you can't make them unsee.;<> Ivan Zhang: In short, it won't end well. Ivan Zhang: I have enough real-life cases to support my point.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->23 did help you, no? ($KnowOldChangPast);//if有問老張故事 Ivan Zhang: She told you about me?;<> Ivan Zhang: Yes, she did help. Ivan Zhang: Not without getting some benefit out of it. Ivan Zhang: What benefit can you offer them? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nothing. Ivan Zhang: Now you see. ->She also helped you because it's the right thing to do. Ivan Zhang: A man could dream. Ivan Zhang: Having worked with her for ages, I'm telling you she's not the type. <<#EndSimple>> ->What's your story? Ivan Zhang: ...I'd rather not tell.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Okay. // ->You're not convincing enough. Ivan Zhang: And I don't need to convince you. Ivan Zhang: This is an offer of help, not a deal. Ivan Zhang: Take it or leave it. I'm OK either way. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Ever heard of the Moonflowers? Ivan Zhang: They can be your way out.;<> Ivan Zhang: They offer protection to all civilians who the government or corporations find inconvenient. Ivan Zhang: Like you.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I didn't cause any inconvenience. Ivan Zhang: Matter of time. ->They will take me in? Ivan Zhang: Of course. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Trust me. You will need them. Thought: My ticket out, huh... Ivan Zhang: There's one condition, though, if you're to join the Moonflowers.;<> Ivan Zhang: You must leave your past life behind. Ivan Zhang: They will give you a new identity, to start over in a place where nobody knows you. Ivan Zhang: This help extends to you and you alone. You can't bring anybody with you.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why? Ivan Zhang: Because it's safer, for you and them alike. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: If someday in the future you need them... Ivan Zhang: Come find me in this restaurant. 4:00 AM, any Wednesday. I'll take you over.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Are you a member of the Moonflowers? Ivan Zhang: No. Ivan Zhang: I attract too much attention as a business owner. They usually keep a safe distance. Ivan Zhang: But they trust me enough for referrals. ->I see. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: There, I said it.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thank you. Ivan Zhang: ... ->Why are you helping me? Ivan Zhang: I've seen too many young men like you. Ivan Zhang: Too many. Sometimes I feel pity for you. Ivan Zhang: I'm not what you'd call a good man. Could use some good deeds to balance my karma. Ivan Zhang: Don't want to go to hell when I croak. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Thank you. Ivan Zhang: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Just remember. You have choices. Ivan Zhang: Now, scram.;<> Thought: ... <> <<#End>>

title: 敲23門5
23大接露2
body: Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: She told you to do this.;<>;<> 23: Yes. Nin Situ: ... 23: Please, don't go. You know she has planned something terrible for you.;<> Nin Situ: ...She told you what Vision really is doing. 23: She hinted. Said it's something you will hate with all your heart, but swallow it nonetheless. 23: By then, she will have found out your nature. 23: Grandma is very confident. Nin Situ: ...Another of her adversity tests.;<> 23: Most likely, but I can't tell what exactly awaits. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Are you part of the test, or a testee like I am?;<> 23: Do you think I could know?;<> 23: ... 23: Maybe I'm both. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I don't get it, 23.;<> Nin Situ: I outplay her tests every single time. Why are you so worried now? Nin Situ: Why are you working so hard to stop me? Even sabotaging the operation? 23: Because you don't get the point of adversity tests.;<> 23: You believe you have seen the worst Grandma can do. 23: No, you're wrong. She can escalate the game indefinitely! 23: Eventually you will lose. 23: Only then will she be done with you. Nin Situ: Of course I know that.;<> 23: I know it as an irrelevant fact. From now on, you will feel it. 23: I have been there. I've seen it. 23: The moment that I had to make a decision against all my principles. 23: Your moment is yet to come. 23: So, for now, I know better than you do.;<> Nin Situ: 23... Nin Situ: ... 23: ...;<> 23: One day she will break you, change you. 23: Most likely, tomorrow. 23: ... 23: But I... 23: I don't want to see you change. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: 23... Nin Situ: Is this worth it? 23: Nin, you know I made plenty decisions that I'm ashamed of.;<> 23: I've cut short lives that should not have been taken. Nin Situ: ... 23: And you know what it did to me. 23: I became obsessed with principles, to make sure I won't go too far into the dark. 23: Even that, I still can't sleep at night. 23: I hate myself for what I am.;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Principles can't protect you.;<> Nin Situ: They are rigid, made to be broken. When they break, you take a heavier hit. Nin Situ: Putting them in the center of your faith doesn't make you better off. Nin Situ: If anything, you will doubt yourself even more. 23: What SHOULD I do then!?;<>;<>;<> 23: How can I be as peaceful as the almighty superhuman Situ with her fluid values? 23: Laws and rules, at least they are definite!;<> 23: Isn't that the whole point? Something definite to fall back on, to stop the doubt. 23: Something that tells me it's not my fault, so that I can sleep at night! 23: I do what I do to get rid of this guilt, this self-hate! 23: You don't think it's a thing because you've never been there yet!!!;<> 23: All your talk about the right thing, about justice, about fluid values! 23: Do you think it makes you invincible!? 23: "Look! They all failed, these losers!";<> 23: Everyone eventually had to face their ugly selves! 23: What makes you think you're any different? 23: When your time comes, you'll—!!! Nin Situ: ...;<> 23: ...;<>;<> <<#End>> //23縮了回去，安靜一下

title: 23大接露3
body: Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I'm not doing this for what's "right". Nin Situ: The point of enforcing justice is justice itself. Nin Situ: All I want is to help more people. Nin Situ: Meanwhile, you want inner peace that allows you to sleep at night. Nin Situ: Neither of these can be achieved by holding onto a set of definite standards. Nin Situ: What we need is to keep understanding ourselves, understanding the world around us. Nin Situ: Make decisions that are "right" for a specific where and when. Nin Situ: That's how you make things "right". 23: ... Nin Situ: Admittedly, there are a lot of grey areas in this process. Nin Situ: I keep doubting myself, all the time. Nin Situ: Every now and then I adjust my concept of "right" as needed. Nin Situ: Doubting myself is an integral part of life. Nin Situ: Every bullet fired, every life taken... Nin Situ: I doubt if it's right all the time. The stress is constant. Nin Situ: But again, this doubt is what makes me feel assured. Nin Situ: If I'm doubting myself, I can assess, adapt, and rectify. Nin Situ: I'm not going to walk a clearly defined path. Nin Situ: That would make me overly trust myself, and justify what I have done wrong. 23: ... 23: ... 23: Well said. 23: ... 23: But what's the point? 23: Talk as much as you want, I'm still what I am.;<>;<> 23: What did you expect? That I admit I'm wrong? That I repent? Nin Situ: I didn't mean... 23: I know I'm on a wrong path, Nin. 23: Always. Nin Situ: ...;<> 23: Still, I am what I am. 23: I know you don't need my protection. 23: I know my motive is entirely selfish. 23: Nonetheless, I want to see you well, see you happy. 23: I'd hate to see the moment that they break you. 23: You're too good to deserve that. 23: You take all the pain and hold it inside... Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: This is my choice. 23: ...I know.;<> 23: ... 23: You know what, Nin?;<> 23: Even as a beholder... 23: The way how hard you try makes me feel bad.;<> 23: I feel like a soft, selfish bastard. Nin Situ: No. You're not— 23: Oh, but I am. 23: ... 23: If Grandma does break you... 23: If she turns you into one of us, a nihilist... 23: Then maybe I can tell myself that I'm not bad, I'm normal. Just look at Nin... 23: We're the same, we're equal. Nin Situ: We're always equal, 23.;<> 23: Or perhaps... 23: Perhaps I don't want to see you beaten. I'm afraid to see your wings snapped.;<> 23: If you give up, I'd have even less of a chance. Nin Situ: ...;<> 23: ... 23: Dammit...;<> 23: Life is tough, Nin. Nin Situ: ...Yeah.;<> Nin Situ: So very tough... 23: ... Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: No one will change me.;<> Nin Situ: When this is all over... Nin Situ: I'll be the same person that I am now. Nin Situ: I promise. 23: ...;<> 23: Can't we hold it for one more day? Grandma says after tomorrow, permanent changes will happen.;<>;<> 23: By then, you won't be able to undo it no matter how hard you try. 23: And you could try as hard as you want. The result will be the same, and you won't have to blame yourself. 23: Circumvent the adversity test by not making a choice at all. Nin Situ: Not making a choice itself is a choice. 23: But... Nin Situ: 23... Nin Situ: There are people being tortured. 23: ... 23: Torture, huh... 23: Sounds bad... Nin Situ: Yeah... Nin Situ: It is bad. It's terrible. 23: The tranq... I'm blacking out.;<>;<>;<> 23: ... 23: Nin... 23: Everything you need is ready. Same old place. 23: The decryption system is customized for you. You don't need me for it. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Thank you, 23. 23: ... 23: Nin... 23: What's the right thing to do, if I could start over? 23: How can I deal with this with peace? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: No matter what we do... Nin Situ: As long as we still care... 23: There won't be peace? Nin Situ: ... 23: I see... 23: But... 23: Damn... 23: We can't just stop caring... <<#End>> //23昏過去，司徒扶助他把23輕輕放下，僵在一個崩潰姿勢一段時間

title: 23大接露結束司徒談話
body: Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: What are you here for?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I wanted to check how you're doing. Nin Situ: Thank you. ->I thought 23 was acting weird. Nin Situ: Sharp eyes. ->Nothing, just passing by. Nin Situ: Huh. <<#EndSimple>> //司徒起身淡出淡入直視待機 <> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: 23 won't be with us tomorrow. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Tomorrow we do as planned? Nin Situ: We must. ->What if she changes her mind? // ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: The tranquilizer will knock her out for at least 24 hours. Nin Situ: She wanted to use it on me and keep me out of the mission. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What for? // ->Luckily you took precautions. Nin Situ: ...Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Grandma is watching me all the time. Nin Situ: Since the beginning of this operation. Nin Situ: She led me to Vision. Our operation is a part of her plan. Nin Situ: Although I wasn't expecting 23 to be part of it...;<> Nin Situ: That wretched woman... Thought: She is angry. Nin Situ: This whole elaborate setup is one of her adversity tests. Nin Situ: 23 wanted to keep me out of it, so that I won't play Grandma's game. //if知道司徒是私冰 <<#If($SiTuRealMaster=2)>> <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Aren't you her own agent? Why is she against you? ($DLStateFloat);//姊所選項 Nin Situ: Exactly because I'm her own agent. She must ensure complete dominance. Nin Situ: Grandma won't stop until she gets what she wants. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: If she's honest to 23, tomorrow will be the final stage of the test.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What will happen? // ->Shall we postpone it? Nin Situ: No. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: 23 said permanent changes will happen after tomorrow. Nin Situ: Something big must be happening, with far-reaching consequences. Nin Situ: We must find out what it is while we have time. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: This is a part of Grandma's plan, too. She knows I simply can't give up. Nin Situ: As to what she prepared for me... Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: There's no way to tell. We must stay alert and be ready to improvise. Nin Situ: If we move fast enough tomorrow, we should be largely alright. Nin Situ: We— Gah!;<> //司徒跪地，摀著身體 Nin Situ: Damn... <<#Simple(8)>> ->How are you holding up? ->Are you really ready for tomorrow? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I'm alright. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: I'll be a lot better by the morning. You should have some rest, too. Nin Situ: We will go over specific tactics in the morning. Thought: She's pale... Nin Situ: And... Nin Situ: I will make an excuse to explain 23's absence. Nin Situ: Don't tell Lawrence. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I see. Nin Situ: Thank you. ->I refuse. ->Don't tell him? Again? ->Why? Nin Situ: Lawrence will be devastated if he knew what 23 did? Nin Situ: We can't risk it, especially now. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He is already very confused. And you want to hide more? Nin Situ: All the more reason to not confuse him any further. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He will never be ready if you don't give him a chance to get ready. Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: What do you think I should do? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Tell him everything. ->I don't know, but lying is not a solution. <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@司徒寧害怕告訴隆生23真相)>> ->A bit of transparency won't hurt. Nin Situ: I'm temporarily keeping him in the dark.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What's the difference? ->Just tell him the truth. <<#EndSimple>> <<@司徒寧害怕告訴隆生23真相>> Nin Situ: I...;<> Nin Situ: I can't.;<> Nin Situ: I can't break his heart. Nin Situ: His life is hard enough. Now he's betrayed by one of the few people he trusts. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: But you're right.;<> Nin Situ: I should tell him everything. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I should have enough trust in him that he can process this information. Nin Situ: Maybe I've been overprotective all this time... Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Exactly like what 23 did to me...;<> Thought: She's genuinely sad. Nin Situ: I may need a bit time to prepare... Nin Situ: But I will tell him everything, soon. Thank you, Jason.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah. // ->That is the right way. Nin Situ: I agree. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> ->Alright, but you must tell him afterwards. Nin Situ: I will. I promise. <<#EndSimple>> ->Alright, but you must tell him afterwards. Nin Situ: I will. I promise. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: OK. So much for tonight. <<#Simple(8)>> ->It's been a long day for you. Thought: She looks weary. ->What do we do about 23? Nin Situ: Leave her to me. Nin Situ: She could use some rest. Nin Situ: The tranquilizer will give her some dreamless sleep.;<> Thought: She loves 23. I can tell. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: ... <<#Simple(10)>> ->What's up? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Am I a heartless person? <> <<@回答司徒是否無情>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Kinda. Nin Situ: I see... Nin Situ: Thank you. <<#Simple(8)>> ->It takes a heartless person to get things done. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: ...Yeah. Nin Situ: Indeed... ->But to your close friends, you're not. <<#GoTo(@司徒無情話題)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->No. <<@司徒無情話題>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I know I'm unreasonably rational.;<> Nin Situ: To me, everything is clear-cut. Black and white, right and wrong. Nin Situ: My judgments aren't always correct, but I make decisions fast and without remorse. Nin Situ: I don't stop simply because something might hurt myself or my friends. Nin Situ: I make no compromise. Nin Situ: However, I don't think I'm particularly demanding moral-wise. Nin Situ: If I think something is wrong, it's usually very wrong. I can't pretend otherwise. Nin Situ: Knowing what needs to be done but not doing it is unimaginable. Nin Situ: I can't find myself any excuse. Nin Situ: I see something within my ability, I must do it. Nin Situ: For that, people call me cold, heartless, on my high horse... Nin Situ: In doing what I do, I bring harm to people who I care about.;<> Thought: She's looking at 23. <<#Simple(12)>> ->This struggle is proof enough that you're not heartless.;<> Thought: She tensed up for a split second, as if never thought of this. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Maybe you're right. Nin Situ: Thank you, Jason. Nin Situ: That makes me feel much better. ->Don't be so hard on yourself. Nin Situ: Maybe.;<> Nin Situ: Or maybe I'm not hard enough. ->I think you said this. There's a cost for doing the right thing. Thought: She tensed up for a split second, as if never thought of this.;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: ...Yes. Very true. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... Thought: A sudden change of vibes... Nin Situ: In fact, I have more in common with Chameleon. Nin Situ: I believe we share essentially the same value system. Nin Situ: The thing is, he wiped the line between right and wrong. Making decisions is a lot easier that way. Nin Situ: I just can't do that. Nin Situ: I don't see myself any better than him. Nin Situ: We are on the same path, it's just that he's further down the road. Nin Situ: I can't judge him, because I've never been where he is. Nin Situ: Didn't 23 say something like that? Nin Situ: I never had to make a life-changing decision like she did... <<#Simple(10)>> ->I don't agree with her. Nin Situ: Don't agree? With what? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That you have to be in her shoes to see her point. Nin Situ: I don't think that's what she meant, but...;<> Nin Situ: I see what you mean, because I have the same belief. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I'm grateful that you agree with me. Nin Situ: In moments like this I really need some affirmation to face myself. ->That facing an impossible challenge will change you. Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: Thanks for the confidence. Nin Situ: I have confidence in myself, too. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Or else I wouldn't make the promise to 23. <<#EndSimple>> ->We will know tomorrow. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Haha, yeah. Nin Situ: The moment of truth is close.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Now, it's really late. Nin Situ: You should go home. ->You mean, the way how you treated 23? ($DLStateFloat);//選過消失 <> Nin Situ: No, in general. <<#GoTo(@回答司徒是否無情)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I should go. Bed time. Nin Situ: Right... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Good night, Jason. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Good night. // ->All will be over by this time tomorrow. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: ...Yeah. Nin Situ: By this time tomorrow. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>> //司徒轉身走到23旁邊蹲下擋住他好像是在照顧他 //這裡交代司徒寧的痛苦 //他說對於理性的自己，一切事務的對錯都是這麼清楚。這無關她是不是個好人，只是當正確答案如此明顯，不去做就會很痛苦。她找不了藉口，她有能力，也能判斷是非。 //被知識詛咒的人，變得厭世享樂主義，要馬徹底摧毀自己的道德感，成為享樂主義。要馬起身行動。所以我跟白壁虎卻是非常相像。 //我並不覺得我優秀，我只是做得到而已，而白壁虎是做不到的人。

title: 第四日內心世界2
body: Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: The way how Situ fought Chameleon was quite an eye opener. Jason Dai: It's like a superhero movie... Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: If she's not bullshitting, I will be able to pull that off, too, with some training. Jason Dai: Then I won't need protection from anybody. Jason Dai: I can totally take care of myself and Fran... Jason Dai: Although... Jason Dai: One-on-one combat is one thing. I need protection against the establishment. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: OWL. Jason Dai: She said we will join OWL sooner than expected. Jason Dai: She made it sound like a bad thing, but I think it's exactly the opposite. Jason Dai: She loathes OWL because she's a helpless idealist. Jason Dai: She can't cope with all those dirty deals OWL makes with corporations under the counter. Jason Dai: I, on the other hand, don't give a flying fuck. Jason Dai: If OWL wants to maintain the status quo, fine by me! Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: So, here is my plan. Once the time comes... Jason Dai: I'll take Situ's referral, join OWL, and cut ties with her right after. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Why cut ties? Jason Dai: You don't have to be a genius to tell she's not popular within OWL. Jason Dai: The kind that just can't stop being a troublemaker. Jason Dai: I don't want to be seen as part of her gang. Jason Dai: That would be counterproductive, right? <<#Simple(8)>> ->But she helped you. Jason Dai: I know. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I'm just cutting ties, not turning against her. Jason Dai: Let's call it keeping a respectful distance. ->I see. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Now you want to join OWL. Jason Dai: Want or not, I didn't have a choice to begin with. Jason Dai: At least now I'm starting to see a positive side. Jason Dai: So, to answer your question, yep, I'm happy to join OWL. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Now you know my plan. Jason Dai: ... //if第三日收在不好路線 <<#If($RelationshipWithMC<-3)>> Jason Dai: Although I don't think you care. //if第一日說指照自己想法行動 <<#If($HelpMCEnding=1)>> Jason Dai: You do what you want to do. Screw my opinions, yeah? <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. // ->I do care about what you think. Jason Dai: You said you don't like me yesterday. Jason Dai: And I said I don't trust you. Jason Dai: Are we having a sudden change of heart now? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I don't like you alright, but I won't intentionally harm you, either. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Why? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Come on, I don't hold any grudge against you. ->No why, I just think. ->Because I'm not a bad guy. <<#EndSimple>> ->You don't have to like someone to care about his opinions. Jason Dai: ...That doesn't make sense. Jason Dai: Say, if we are enemies, why would you care about my opinions? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Maybe I feel sorry for occupying your body. ->No why, I just think. ->Because I'm not a bad guy. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: So, I'm supposed to trust you in return? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Suit yourself. ->I don't need your trust. ->Well, no one's stopping you. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: OK. Jason Dai: About the plan I mentioned... Jason Dai: That I'd join OWL then cut ties with Situ. <<#GoTo(@問玩家怎麼想主角預設選擇)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Thought so. Jason Dai: Then I won't waste my breath on you. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Did you want to tell me something? Jason Dai: Never mind. I have nothing to say now. ->Yeah, makes life easier for us both. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Yeah. Jason Dai: It's about time... <<#Simple(6)>> ->That was quick. Jason Dai: Don't you appreciate? Jason Dai: I don't enjoy being locked in the same room with you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Get lost. <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> <> <<@問玩家怎麼想主角預設選擇>> Jason Dai: What do you think? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What I think doesn't matter. You've made up your mind. ($DLStateFloat);//這兩個選項，選了一個就都消失 ->What do you care? ($DLStateFloat);//這兩個選項，選了一個就都消失 <> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: You're the only one that I can discuss anything with. //if關係不好 <<#If($RelationshipWithMC<-3)>> Jason Dai: Maybe you don't like or trust me, but... <<#GoTo(@跳過關係好認為玩家在乎自己)>> <<#EndIf>> //if關係好 Jason Dai: I believe you care about me and Fran. <<@跳過關係好認為玩家在乎自己>> Jason Dai: I've been possessed many times, but you're the first who bothers to talk to me. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: You know what? Jason Dai: Even if I'm not possessed, the Jason Dai people see from day to day isn't me. Jason Dai: That Jason doesn't know what I know. He's not aware of being possessed. Jason Dai: I am the real Jason but I have never really lived. Jason Dai: I've been locked in this room since the dawn of time. Jason Dai: Before you came— Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Sorry, I wandered off. Jason Dai: Let's get back to the subject. Jason Dai: I will join OWL then cut ties with Situ. <<#GoTo(@問玩家怎麼想主角預設選擇)>> ->It's a good idea. Jason Dai: Is it? Good. <> ->Sorry, I wouldn't agree with you. Jason Dai: What do you think I should do, then? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Join OWL, but keep in touch with Situ.;<> Jason Dai: Why? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She's a powerful ally. ->She's an upright person. ->You can't just ditch who helped you. <<#EndSimple>> //if說服隆生走好路線 <<#If($RonEnding=1)>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I see you're a true fan of hers... Jason Dai: I could tell from the conversation you had with Lawrence. <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: There is only one scenario where I wouldn't cut ties with Situ. Jason Dai: That she and what she stands for could protect me and Fran. Jason Dai: You know as much as do, it's not gonna happen. Jason Dai: If I can't change them, I'll join them. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Why? Jason Dai: Change comes with conflict, sometimes casualties. Jason Dai: Being a good servant, though, is much safer. Jason Dai: That way, I not only avoid confrontation, but also get a cut from whatever privilege they have. Jason Dai: That is why I want to join OWL. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't you feel ashamed, being a servant? ->For that, you'd rather be their dog? Jason Dai: Survival first. Talk about honor later. Jason Dai: I respect Situ for what she is. Jason Dai: I'm no hero. All I want is my own life back. Jason Dai: Call me whatever you like. I'm prostituting myself? No problem. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->You can choose to do nothing. Jason Dai: I tried. Jason Dai: It's been my motto. Don't fuck with the world, and the world doesn't fuck with me. Jason Dai: How did that work for me? You have seen it yourself. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: That lecture Yuna Hsu gave us. I didn't get her point back then. Jason Dai: Now I get it. Jason Dai: Like she said, she and I are the same kind of people. Jason Dai: We are willing to sacrifice ourselves for the people we love, but we are not able to do anything for them. Jason Dai: She saw that in Fran and I. Jason Dai: So she offered me a precious piece of advice. Jason Dai: Don't fight against the power that be. Join them. Outplay them. Jason Dai: I'm enlightened. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Outplay them, to what extent? Jason Dai: What do you mean? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->In a wicked system, you're never safe. ->There's always someone above you. They keep you down. Jason Dai: Why would they? I'm one of them. I defend the system that represents their interest. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->A wicked system wouldn't care. You're just one pawn out of many. ->There are factions. You're always in someone's way. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Maybe you're right, but it's based on one condition. Jason Dai: That the system itself is rotten to the core. Jason Dai: So much so that all rules are dysfunctional. Everyone is free to use any dirty trick. Jason Dai: There's no such thing as check and balance. A dictatorship run by a selected few. Jason Dai: That is not true. We live in civilized, modern times. Jason Dai: The water might be murky in places, but generally clear. We have the rule of law. <<#Simple(8)>> ->After everything, you still think that? Jason Dai: After everything, I see that I am too weak. This is the root of the problem. Jason Dai: As to the world... ->How could you call it the rule of law? Jason Dai: Criminals are punished. Jason Dai: Even government officials get punished for wrongdoing from time to time. Jason Dai: So. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I believe this society is generally healthy. <<#Simple(8)>> ->They almost killed Fran. These corporations are above the law. Jason Dai: Chances are Yan wanted to kill Fran, not Vision. Jason Dai: And if this gets exposed, Yan will answer to the law. ->They're buying children for human experiments. Jason Dai: For human trafficking, they will be punished if the law enforcement is aware of it. Jason Dai: As to human experiment, we don't have any proof. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I know that you think the system stinks. Jason Dai: But everyone approaches it from a different angle. Jason Dai: First and foremost, you and I, neither of us knows enough to tell if the system is actually that bad. Jason Dai: Moreover, we have different priorities. Jason Dai: You value the freedom of speech and opinions. The only thing I care about is my quiet way of life. Jason Dai: You insist all crimes must be punished. I believe punishments are only due when the evidence is piled high enough. Jason Dai: I see what you see, but I wouldn't call the system rotten beyond salvage yet. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You're naïve. Jason Dai: No, you are naïve, dear friend. Jason Dai: You're an idealist, constantly seeking the ideal world that doesn't exist. Jason Dai: Of course you can't find it. Jason Dai: Because the real world is not ideal. See it as what it is. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Is it any better to just give in? ->I'm not seeking perfection. Improvement. I want improvement. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I'm not talking about razing the system and starting over. I'm trying to improve it. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Still, you're making yourself an enemy of the power that be. Jason Dai: From there comes waves of retaliation, the thing that I've been trying to avoid all along. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Have you thought that you can't indefinitely "grow into it"? ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Enough about this subject. Jason Dai: This is not a debate. Jason Dai: You've made your point clear enough. Jason Dai: I hold onto my faith in the current system. Jason Dai: Although... Jason Dai: I will consider what you said. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Thank you for you valuable input. //if好感度不高 <<#If($RelationshipWithMC<-3)>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: We've never had a friendly relationship. Jason Dai: But you're still willing to have this hearty talk. That means something. <<#EndIf>> <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> ->This is for survival. I can do it for as long as needed. Jason Dai: Yeah. Jason Dai: I'm glad you understand. <<#EndSimple>> ->Don't join OWL. Take Ivan Zhang's offer and get a new life.;<> Jason Dai: ...Why? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That would give Fran true freedom. <<@辯論尊重芳是要怎麼選>> Jason Dai: She said I should respect her choices. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She also said she'd respect your choices. Jason Dai: ...Then she'd reshape herself to fit into the future that I chose. Jason Dai: What do you think she would do? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She will search all over the world for you. ->I don't know. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... ->You know she'd never be a puppet, not willingly. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I know. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't you think she deserves the life that she wants? ->And you still want to break her heart? Jason Dai: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: The Fran I know is a strong woman. Jason Dai: Being excessively considerate would be an insult to her. Jason Dai: She said it's her choice, I trust she can handle it. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: If one day I choose to leave her... Jason Dai: ...It must be a decision for my own interest. Jason Dai: For example, I can't keep Fran with me at the cost of her freedom. Jason Dai: It's me who decides whether she is losing her freedom, not her. <<#Simple(8)>> ->So, you're changing your mind? Jason Dai: No. I stand by my decision. Jason Dai: But... ->Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@跟士傑說還是逃跑好路線結尾>> Jason Dai: I will think about it. //if好感度不高 <<#If($RelationshipWithMC<-3)>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: We've never had a friendly relationship. Jason Dai: But you're still willing to have this hearty talk. That means something. <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: Thank you. <<#End>> ->Don't trust OWL. It can't guarantee your safety. Jason Dai: Are the Moonflowers any better? <<#Simple(8)>> ->You already know about OWL's sponsors. ->OWL won't hesitate to kill you. The Moonflowers? You don't know yet. Jason Dai: Even so... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: There are things I can't lose. Jason Dai: I can't just give up everything for a threat that we do not know if it's real yet. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I can relate. // ->What about Fran? Are you really willing to make her a caged bird? Jason Dai: ... <<#GoTo(@辯論尊重芳是要怎麼選)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: We don't agree with each other, but I see where you're coming from... <<#GoTo(@跟士傑說還是逃跑好路線結尾)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Stand your ground.;<> Jason Dai: ...What? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Be it Vision or OWL, you just don't surrender. Jason Dai: Easier said than done. What am I supposed to do, eh? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Whatever you do, don't pick a side. Jason Dai: Survive on my own? No way! Jason Dai: And you're not answering my question. Jason Dai: Do you have a real solution to offer, other than sticking it to the man? <<#GoTo(@主角問玩家怎麼選結局玩家給不好回應)>> ->Be a pain in their asses. Give them hell. Jason Dai: Give me something actionable. Jason Dai: Or else do you just want me to be a terrorist? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Pretty much. ->Let them know that they're fucking with the wrong man. <<#EndSimple>> //if隆生好結局。 <<#If($RonEnding=1)>> Jason Dai: That's not what you told Lawrence... <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: No. Jason Dai: That doesn't help me at all. <<#Simple(8)>> ->They push and kick you. Don't you wanna fight back? Jason Dai: No. Jason Dai: Because I have nothing to gain from it, except for more beating. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@主角問玩家怎麼選結局玩家給不好回應>> Jason Dai: I don't care about dignity of justice. As long as Fran is well, I'm all good. Jason Dai: Standing up to any of these forces defeats that purpose. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Maybe I shouldn't talk about this with you. //if好感度不高 <<#If($RelationshipWithMC<-3)>> Jason Dai: I mean, you never cared much about my interest. <<#EndIf>> <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Run away on your own. Don't rely on any of them. Jason Dai: Are you nuts? Jason Dai: I can't outrun OWL, not by myself. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You don't know that. At least give it a try. ->Or else what? Sit on your ass and wait for things to happen? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I have plenty of options. Jason Dai: It's just you who are obsessed with the idea of independence. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Trust yourself, not them. ->It's just you who are obsessed with the illusion of options. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@主角問玩家怎麼選結局玩家給不好回應)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Maybe it's too soon to think about exit plans. Jason Dai: Tomorrow may bring us new opportunities. Who knows? Jason Dai: ... <<#End>>

Tell Situ about Lawrence's trick the first thing tomorrow morning. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You have my promise. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Thank you... ->No, I can't betray him. Jason Dai: Please. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //if不合謀，也不答應隱瞞 <<#If($RonEnding=2.3)>> Jason Dai: You're doing the right thing. Tell Situ about Lawrence's trick the first thing tomorrow morning. title: 麵店被叫醒(MBU)2
body: Ivan Zhang: Not expecting to see you again so soon. Ivan Zhang: And you're hanging out with 23... <<#Simple(10)>> ->Small world, huh. Ivan Zhang: Hmph. ->I live practically next door. Ivan Zhang: Not you. Jason Dai lives next door. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Right. Ivan Zhang: Hmph. ->What's the difference? I am Jason Dai. Ivan Zhang: No. You're something else using his body. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Oi. Ivan Zhang: You. Ivan Zhang: What the hell do you want to do? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What do you mean? Ivan Zhang: You know what I mean. ->I'm investigating Vision Corp. Ivan Zhang: That much I know. It's not what I'm asking. <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: This is not your body. Ivan Zhang: What do you want to do with it? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm trying to help this body's rightful owner. Ivan Zhang: Help? Ivan Zhang: How do you know you're helping? Ivan Zhang: Maybe he doesn't want you to mess with big corps. <<#Simple(6)>> ->I can communicate with him. Ivan Zhang: ...You can? Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Creepy, you and your kind. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Why do you care? Ivan Zhang: Why? Ivan Zhang: You're abducting a living body! <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Does Situ know about it? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->They don't know I'm in control. Ivan Zhang: Meaning they know that Jason Dai can be controlled, huh... Ivan Zhang: Hmph. ->Guess. Ivan Zhang: Most likely they don't. Ivan Zhang: Or else they won't work with things they don't understand. <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Listen. Ivan Zhang: I don't know what your purpose is, nor do I care. Ivan Zhang: But this body has an owner, and he has a life. Ivan Zhang: Please, don't ruin it. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Why do you care so much about him? Ivan Zhang: Because I know how it feels to be possessed. //if玩家有說自己控制 <<#If($ToldChangYouControl=0)>> Ivan Zhang: Possessed by you, in case you forgot. <<#EndIf>> Ivan Zhang: I'm no good man, but I do have a heart. Ivan Zhang: What happened to me can't happen to him. ->I won't do that to him. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: Guess I have to trust you. ->I have my plan. Don't get in my way. Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: You... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: You know what? I've seen plenty of folks like you. <<#Simple(6)>> ->You've seen a lot of M.B.Us? Ivan Zhang: I mean people like you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: You're doing OWL's dirty work because you don't have a choice. They need you for snitch or muscles, yeah? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sort of. // ->I'm not forced. Ivan Zhang: Of course they won't force you. Situ and 23 and generally good people. <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: And let me guess. It involves some huge ass corporation? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I can't tell you. Ivan Zhang: Yeah. You don't have a reason to trust me and I'm alright with that. Ivan Zhang: However. ->How do you know? Ivan Zhang: I know them long enough to know their line of work. ->Wrong. Ivan Zhang: Bullshit. <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Have you thought about what happens afterwards? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Afterwards? Nothing. Ivan Zhang: No. <<#GoTo(@老張說明任務後會怎樣2)>> ->Do you mean there's something bad waiting for me? Ivan Zhang: Pretty much. <<#GoTo(@老張說明任務後會怎樣2)>> ->Never thought about it. <<#GoTo(@老張說明任務後會怎樣2)>> ->I work for them in exchange for protection. Ivan Zhang: Before your operation ends, yes. Ivan Zhang: What about afterwards? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'll be a free man afterwards. Ivan Zhang: I like your optimism, but things don't often end that well. ->What could happen? <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<@老張說明任務後會怎樣2>> Ivan Zhang: ... Ivan Zhang: They will move on to other cases. You're forgotten. Ivan Zhang: Whatever aftermath this operation has, you will be the one footing the bill. <<#Simple(6)>> ->What if there's no aftermath? Ivan Zhang: Ha! I've lived long enough and never seen one job wrapping up aftermath-free. Ivan Zhang: Think about it yourself. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: What if OWL doesn't want to leave you alone? Or whatever force you're fucking with fucks back? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'll turn to Situ and 23 for help. Ivan Zhang: Can you even get hold of them by then? Ivan Zhang: Let's say you could. How high do you think you are, on their ladder of priorities? Ivan Zhang: Do you really believe they will put down whatever they're doing and come to your rescue? Thought: Situ will not care that much about me... ->What are you getting at? Say it already. <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: I'm saying that you— Ivan Zhang: No. Jason Dai needs an exit plan. Ivan Zhang: Corporations rule this world. Once they see you, you can't make them unsee. Ivan Zhang: In short, it won't end well. Ivan Zhang: I have enough real-life cases to support my point. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->23 did help you, no? ($KnowOldChangPast);//if有問老張故事 Ivan Zhang: She told you about me? Ivan Zhang: Yes, she did help. Ivan Zhang: Not without getting some benefit out of it. Ivan Zhang: What benefit can you offer them? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nothing. Ivan Zhang: Now you see. ->She also helped you because it's the right thing to do. Ivan Zhang: A man could dream. Ivan Zhang: Having worked with her for ages, I'm telling you she's not the type. <<#EndSimple>> ->What's your story? Ivan Zhang: ...I'd rather not tell. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Okay. // ->You're not convincing enough. Ivan Zhang: And I don't need to convince you. Ivan Zhang: This is an offer of help, not a deal. Ivan Zhang: Take it or leave it. I'm OK either way. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Ever heard of the Moonflowers? Ivan Zhang: They can be you and Jason Dai's way out. Ivan Zhang: They offer protection to all civilians who the government or corporations find inconvenient. Ivan Zhang: Like you. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I didn't cause any inconvenience. Ivan Zhang: Matter of time. ->They will take me in? Ivan Zhang: Of course. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Trust me. You will need them. Thought: My ticket out, huh... Ivan Zhang: There's one condition, though, if you're to join the Moonflowers. Ivan Zhang: Jason Dai must leave his past life behind. Not you. You don't really have a life. Ivan Zhang: They will give him a new identity, to start over in a place where nobody knows him. Ivan Zhang: This help extends to him alone. He can't bring anybody along. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why? Ivan Zhang: Because it's safer, for him and them alike. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: If someday in the future you need them... Ivan Zhang: Come find me in this restaurant. 4:00 AM, any Wednesday. I'll take you over. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Are you a member of the Moonflowers? Ivan Zhang: No. Ivan Zhang: I attract too much attention as a business owner. They usually keep a safe distance. Ivan Zhang: But they trust me enough for referrals. ->I see. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: There, I said it. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thank you. Ivan Zhang: ... ->Why are you helping me? //if有說自己控制老張+沒救阿翰 <<#If($ToldChangYouControl=0&&$WrongSyringe=false)>> Ivan Zhang: I'm helping Dai, not you. Ivan Zhang: Don't forget, you owe me a life. <<#GoTo(@跳過老張鑑定有說沒說好不好)>> <<#EndIf>> //if有說自己控制老張+有救阿翰 <<#If($ToldChangYouControl=0&&$WrongSyringe=true)>> Ivan Zhang: You didn't kill Albert. I owe you this much. Ivan Zhang: A little bit of knowledge. Not enough to pay my debt, but I suppose it helps. Ivan Zhang: And I'd help Dai, even without Albert. <<#GoTo(@跳過老張鑑定有說沒說好不好)>> <<#EndIf>> //if不符合以上兩種情況 Ivan Zhang: I'm helping Dai, not you. <<@跳過老張鑑定有說沒說好不好>> Ivan Zhang: I've been possessed myself. We're birds of a feather. Ivan Zhang: I'm not what you'd call a good man. Could use some good deeds to balance my karma. Ivan Zhang: Don't want to go to hell when I croak. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Thank you. Ivan Zhang: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ivan Zhang: Just remember. You have choices. Ivan Zhang: Now, scram. Thought: ... <<#End>>

title: 麵攤3
body: Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: ...Shall I quit this operation?;<> 23: ... 23: *Sigh* 23: Lawrence.;<> Lawrence Chang: ... 23: Listen. 23: You're a smart boy, a quick learner.;<> 23: More importantly, you have a heart of gold.;<> 23: I know this sounds cheesy, but you have no idea what a gem you are. 23: It's easy to be nice when life is easy.;<> 23: Very few could stay nice when life beats them to a pulp. 23: This is the quality that Nin is constantly looking for. 23: The necessary quality for her successor. She wants to build a legacy.;<> 23: I have no doubt, you are the one.;<> Lawrence Chang: ... 23: Your problem is that you're too easily influenced. That temper may lead you astray.;<> 23: For this reason, we pay close attention to the state of your mind. 23: Nin wants to treat you as her equal.;<> 23: She has much to teach, but she doesn't want to be a mentor, your superior. You need to figure things out yourself. 23: Theories and values are cheap enough to talk about. You have no idea how much they weight until you actually need them.;<> 23: I can give you lectures, but you won't understand the point. You may even fight me over it.;<> 23: ... 23: Nin wouldn't tell you what I did. She doesn't believe in lectures.;<> 23: You must discover it on your own. The process itself counts. 23: Sorry. I just couldn't help myself.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: I...;<> 23: Enough. 23: ... 23: Lawrence. 23: You need more time to train your spirit and mind.;<> 23: ... 23: Nin and I, we are proud of you.;<> 23: Keep that in mind.;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Yeah.;<>;<> 23: ... 23: ...;<> 23: As to Nin's plan of negotiating with Vision...;<> 23: I hate to let them go as much as you do. 23: But think about it, don't we need to consider the safety of their test subjects?;<> 23: If I were the boss, I'd be paralyzed. I can't choose between the two. 23: Let's just say, I'm so grateful that I'm not the one making decisions. 23: Whatever Nin chooses, she has to live with the consequences.;<> 23: She cares about consequences way more than the average person. The stress is excruciating.;<> 23: Still, she pushes on, making more decisions, taking more consequences. 23: She's been doing it for a long, long time.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Yeah.;<> 23: ...;<> <> <<#End>>

title: 麵攤
body: //23一直在看手機 23: Hey there.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Hey, Jason. Thought: Lawrence doesn't look very happy.;<> Lawrence Chang: I don't know why but 23 insists on this noodle house. 23: Been here before? Is it any good?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Fran and I come here all the time. 23: The owner must know you. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I guess. ->I don't know. <<#EndSimple>> ->Why ask? 23: Curiosity. <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Hey, 23.;<> 23: What?;<> Lawrence Chang: ...How exactly does Nin want to do this negotiation? Lawrence Chang: Just meet up with them in person? Isn't that dangerous? Can we ask OWL to— 23: Do we have to talk about this now?;<> Lawrence Chang: ... 23: ...;<> 23: Okay.;<> 23: If, hypothetically, that is... 23: If Nin does have a negotiation with Vision, she'd do it through a middleman. <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Who wants to be the middleman in a deal like this? 23: The only kind. 23: Those who want to teach Vision a lesson. ->Why not meet them in person? 23: So that one of OWL's top sponsors knows there's an agent against them? 23: That would be stupid. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Most likely, she will ask the Moonflowers to step in.;<> 23: ... 23: You know about the Moonflowers, no?;<> Lawrence Chang: Yeah, my old gang used to trade with them all the time.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I've heard about them. 23: Good. ->Are they real? 23: Real, but not like what the government told you about. ->Never heard of them. 23: The Free Market Act? The Moonflower Movement? Heard any of these?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes. 23: Good.;<> ->Nope. ->What? 23: Youngsters these days. What happened to your education?;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->You're not much older. 23: Tsk. You don't know the Moonflowers. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Not very long ago, this used to be a freer country.;<>;<> 23: Demonstrations and marches were allowed, until this nationwide movement got out of control. 23: Demonstration turned into riot, then crackdown. 23: And there was a curfew that lasted for a year. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Ah, I remember. The Moonflower Movement. 23: Your history teacher can die in peace. ->Oh, the Moonflower Riot. 23: Riot, huh? <<#Simple(6)>> ->Is there anything wrong? 23: Nothing. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: The brainwashed generation. ->So, who's the bad guy really? The government or the rioters? 23: What do you think? 23: Politics are complicated. Look for your own answer. ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Anyway, a huge number of people were arrested.;<> 23: Meanwhile, quite a portion of them burrowed deep. <> <<#GoTo(@跳過說明組成蘭花黨的人是誰)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> 23: Most of them are participants of the Moonflower Movement. <<@跳過說明組成蘭花黨的人是誰>> 23: They are forming guerrilla forces against the government and big corps. 23: Sometimes sabotage operations, too. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Kinda like a rebellion army? 23: Army? Ha!;<>;<> 23: Just a bunch of civilians fooling around and feeling good about it. <> <<@蘭花黨沒有要推翻政府喔>> 23: Also, they never thought about overturning the government.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Because they know they can't. 23: Half right. ->Then what are they up against? 23: This era, I guess.;<> 23: These idealist fools somehow believe they can stop the world from where it's heading and turn it around. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: Anyway, it's far more than overturning a government or two. 23: Oh, I wandered off. ->Sounds like terrorists. 23: Depends on your perspective.;<> <<#GoTo(@蘭花黨沒有要推翻政府喔)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23: They will do anything to just give Vision a stab. Lawrence Chang: Is it possible that they somehow decide to publish everything we know, against Nin's will?;<>;<> 23: Is that what you're counting on? Lawrence Chang: ...;<> 23: No. Nin won't give them everything.;<>;<> 23: They just need a portion to make Vision believe we have more. 23: Nin's name won't appear throughout the process. 23: From Vision's perspective, they're dealing with a bunch of blackmailing terrorists.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Doesn't sound very reliable. 23: I agree.;<> 23: But we have played the same trick enough times to know it works. 23: Given Nin's job and the things she wants... 23: She needs a public face to communicate with big corps and take consequences.;<> ->But we don't have any proof yet. 23: We are not even sure if this human experiment is real. 23: Tomorrow we will know. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //老張走進畫面，淡入淡出 <<#End>>
